Provinces and Oudh, Jan., 1880, to examine the working of the salt department; fellow of the Madras University, March, 1882; on duty to Orissa to report on the system of salt administration, Jan., 1884; on special duty at Madras in connection with the abkari committee, March, 1884; additional member of the board of revenue in addition to his duties as commissioner of salt revenue, Feb., 1886; member of the finance committee under the government of India, April, 1886; fourth member of board of revenue and commissioner of salt and abkari revenue, April, 1887; second member of the board of revenue, &c., Jan., 1889, and first member, Oct., 1889; C.I.E., May, 1889; acting commissioner of land revenue, June, 1890; additional member of legislative council, Dec., 1890, to April, 1891; commissioner of land revenue, Madras, April, 1891.

BLISSETT, T., late Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the Indian telegraph department, 1st March, 1859; assistant superintendent, 1st grade, Jan., 1866; superintendent, Arracan division, Aug., 1869; personal assistant to director-general, 1873-75; served subsequently in the Dacca and Scinde divisions, and in the store department, and from 1882 in Bombay; 1st grade superintendent, Aug., 1883; retired, Jan., 1889.

BLOOD, BAGOT WILLIAM, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1866, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; superintendent of way and works, Rajputana State Railway, Nov., 1879; superintending engineer, 2nd class, supernumerary, Dec., 1890.

BLOOMFIELD, ALFRED, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission as ensign, March, 1856; served in the Central Provinces from July, 1864, as assistant commissioner; assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, March, 1867; deputy commissioner, July, 1870; inspector-general of registration, superintendent of stamps, and commissioner of excise, Oct., 1876; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Aug., 1881; 1st class, Aug., 1889; author of 'Notes on the Baigas of the Central Provinces,' 1885.

BLYTH, VEN. GEORGE FRANCIS POPHAM, M.A., late Ecclesiastical Department, Burma.—Archdeacon of Rangoon, Oct., 1879; retired, 1887.

BLYTH, WILLIAM DUNBAR, M.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at Liverpool Collegiate Institution, and Queen's College, Cork; appointed to

the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1874; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed officiating junior secretary, board of revenue, Aug., 1879; officiating deputy collector of customs, Calcutta, July, 1880; officiating under-secretary, government of Bengal, judicial, political, and appointments departments in 1881 and 1882; under-secretary, government of Bengal, financial department, April, 1882; officiating chief secretary, government of Bengal, April, 1886; officiating inspector-general of registration, July, 1886; 1st inspector of registration offices, Oct., 1886; inspector-general of registration, April, 1887; officiating magistrate and collector, March, 1890; confirmed, Nov., 1890.

BODDAM, WELBY WROUGHTON, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Punjab.—First commission, 12th Dec., 1851; arrived in India, 2nd July, 1852; served with the military police, 1857-61; district superintendent of Lahore police, Feb., 1861; deputy inspector-general March, 1878; promoted to 1st grade, May, 1881; officiating inspector-general, Punjab, March, 1883, and again, Feb., 1885; deputy inspector-general of police, 1st grade, Rawalpindi, March, 1887; retired, March, 1888.

BODDY, OWEN VIDAL, Major, R.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Arrived in India, 11th Feb., 1873; assistant engineer, April, 1873; executive engineer, March, 1883; employed in the military works branch, government of India, Aug., 1887, to Feb., 1888; deputy chief engineer and under secretary in the public works department, Feb., 1891.

BOILEAU, HENRY, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C.—Served under the military department from May, 1865, to April, 1873, when he was appointed officiating assistant commissioner, Burma; on special duty to Mandalay, May, 1873; assistant commissioner, 4th grade, and superintendent of immigration, Sept., 1874; also sub-registrar of deeds, Rangoon, Aug., 1875; on special duty in connection with the Imperial assemblage, Delhi, Nov., 1876; private secretary and aide-de-camp to lieutenant-governor of Bengal, Jan., 1877; cantonment magistrate, Dinapore, and small cause court judge, July, 1878; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Aug., 1885, continuing to be employed as cantonment magistrate; deputy commissioner, March, 1887; 2nd grade, April, 1890.

BOLSTER, J. A., Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India office, 22nd Feb., 1872.

BOLTON, CHARLES WALTER, Bengal C.S.—Educated at University College School, London, Royal College, Mauritius, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 7th Oct., 1872; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, Oct., 1872; officiating political agent, Hill Tipperah, April, 1877; under secretary, government of Bengal, March, 1879; officiating under secretary, government of India, home, revenue, and agricultural department, Feb., 1880; on deputation as under secretary in the judicial, political, and appointments departments, government of Bengal, Feb., 1883; officiating secretary, government of Bengal,

revenue and general departments, in addition to his own duties, March, 1884; officiating magistrate and collector, April, 1884; officiating inspector-general of registration, Oct., 1884; senior marriage registrar, Calcutta, Nov., 1884; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, May, 1885; magistrate and collector, Sept., 1888; officiating secretary to the board of revenue, July, 1889, and again, March, 1891.

BOMPAS, CECIL HENRY, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Westminster and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

BONAVIA, EMANUEL, Brigade Surgeon, M.D., late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 4th August, 1857; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1857; served under the military department up to Dec., 1861, and was present at the capture of Lucknow, March, 1858, and in the Trans-Gogra campaign; assistant civil surgeon, Lucknow, Dec., 1861; on special duty, May, 1866; health officer, Lucknow, and superintendent of museum, observatory, and agricultural society's garden, April, 1867; rank of surgeon, Aug., 1869; was engaged for some time in 1872 under the military department in analysing drinking water; principal of medical school at Balrámpur, in addition to other duties, Jan., 1873; officiating sanitary commissioner, Oudh, Sept. to Nov., 1874, and again Jan. to March, 1877, also officiating as inspector-general of prisons, Oudh; officiating civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Fyzabad, Dec., 1877; civil surgeon, Etawah, Dec., 1881; retired, April, 1888; has published works on date culture in India, and 'The Villager's Domestic Medicine,' 1885.

BONHAM-CARTER, NORMAN, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

BONUS, ARTHUR RIVERS, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Clifton College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to Bombay civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India 17th Jan., 1887; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1887; transferred to Burma as assistant commissioner, Aug., 1889.

BONUS, JOSEPH, Major-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—First commission, Bombay Engineers, June, 1855; arrived in India, March, 1857; during the mutiny served at the siege and storming of Jhansi, the capture of Calpee and Gwalior, and various other operations and engagements (medal and clasp); joined the Bombay public works department as assistant engineer, June, 1859, and was employed on the Bombay harbour defences, Oct., 1860; from Sept., 1861, served as deputy consulting engineer for railways in Sind; officiating deputy consulting engineer and assistant secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, railway branch, March, 1870; from Nov., 1871, served as superintending engineer on Indus Valley state railway, and officiated as engineer-in-chief, Aug., 1872; superintending engineer, 3rd grade, July, 1873; engineer-in-chief, Sindia state railway, Sept., 1876; transferred to Punjab

northern state railway, May, 1877; officiating consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Lahore, July-Oct., 1878; engineer-in-chief, Pindi-Peshawur section, Oct., 1879; medal for services in the Afghan War, 1879-80; superintending engineer, 1st class, April, 1881; acting consulting engineer for railways, and joint secretary to government, Bombay, with temporary rank of chief engineer, 3rd class, May, 1882; and again Aug., 1885, to April, 1886; senior deputy consulting engineer and under secretary to government, railway branch, from Nov., 1883; chief engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; retired, Nov., 1888.

BOOTH, WILLIAM, M.A., Bengal Educational Department.—Served in the department from 27 Nov., 1876, as professor in the presidency college, and officiating principal, Hooghly College; principal Dacca College, June, 1884; class 3 of educational department, July, 1886; professor of physics, Presidency College, Nov., 1890.

BOSANQUET, OSWALD VIVIAN, Madras C.S.—Educated at Clifton College and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras Civil Service after open competition of 1885; arrived in Madras, 17th Sept., 1887, and served in the revenue survey department, and as assistant to collector and magistrate; officiating political assistant, 3rd class, and 2nd assistant to resident at Hyderabad, and assistant secretary for Berar, May, 1890.

BOUGHEY, GEORGE FLETCHER OTTLEY, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, June, 1862; arrived in India, 5th Jan., 1865; served in the Bhotan expedition, 1865-66 (medal & clasp); joined the public works department, Nov., 1869, as assistant engineer, railway branch, N.W. Provinces; executive engineer, Rajputana railway, Sept., 1870; deputy consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, May, 1877; officiating assistant director, state railways, central system, 1878; assistant secretary, railway branch, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1879; served in the Afghan War, 1878-79-80 (medal); personal assistant to chief engineer, buildings and roads and railway branches, N.W. Provinces, Aug., 1880; officiating manager, Nizam's state railway, Jan., 1881; manager, Indus Valley and Kandahar railway, Jan., 1882; 1st class, state railway revenue establishment, Oct., 1882; manager, Eastern Bengal State Railway, Nov., 1885; officiating manager, North-Western Railway, April, 1891.

BOULNOIS, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B., late Punjab Chief Court.—Educated at King's Coll., London, and London University; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 30th April, 1855, and in the same year was admitted advocate of the supreme court of Fort William, in Bengal; practised at the bar of supreme court, 1855 to 1860; professor of law in the presidency college, Calcutta, 1857-61, and again in 1882-3; judge of the court of small causes, Bengal, 1860, and first judge of the court, 1861; officiating secretary to the government of Bengal, in the legislative department, 1862-4; officiating deputy secretary to the government of India, legislative department, 1864; appointed judge of the chief court, Punjab, in Feb., 1866, and was senior judge in the court from 1867 until his retirement in 1877; in 1881 was appointed

reporter of appeal cases, which appointment he still holds; member of the senate of the Punjab university college, 1869, and fellow of the Punjab university, 1882; editor of law reports in the supreme court, 1856-60; joint author of 'Notes on Customary Law,' by Boulnois and Rattigan, 1876.

BOULTON, CHARLES FREDERICK, Colonel, I.S.C., late Sind Commission.—Arrived in India, 6th Aug., 1852; on military duty up to Sept., 1856, when he was appointed acting assistant collector and magistrate, Satara; from 1860 served in Sind as deputy collector and magistrate; superintendent, Bombay, Baroda, and C.I. railway police, Jan.-Aug., 1866; served subsequently in Sind as deputy collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and acting judge and sessions judge; manager of encumbered estates in the Karachi, Hyderabad, and Shikarpur collectorates, March, 1877; collector and magistrate, Dec., 1879; acting commissioner in Sind, March-June, 1884.

BOURDILLON, JAMES AUSTIN, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1870; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, Dec., 1870; officiating under secretary, government of India, financial department, Sept., 1874, and subsequently in the home department, March, 1876; officiating assistant secretary, government of Bengal, Dec., 1875; from Aug., 1876, served in the registration department as inspector of registration offices, and officiating inspector-general; deputy superintendent of census, Dec., 1880; inspector-general of registration, Dec., 1881; magistrate and collector, April, 1887.

BOUSTEAD, R., Surgeon-Major, F.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), late Bombay Medical Department.—Civil surgeon, Thana, 21st Jan., 1876; acted as superintendent of vaccination, 1876; superintendent of Matheran, Feb., 1882; acted as surgeon, Gokuldas Tejpal Hospital, 1883; civil surgeon, Ahmednagar, July, 1887; retired, Feb., 1880.

BOUTFLOWER, WILLIAM NOLAN, B.A., Education Department, N.W. Provinces.—Served in the education department from 9th Aug., 1869, as professor in the Agra College, and officiated as principal, July-Dec., 1872; 3rd professor in the Muir Central College, Allahabad, Oct., 1874; officiating inspector of schools, July, 1875, to May, 1876; professor of mathematics, Muir College, March, 1876; 3rd grade of educational officers, April, 1878; officiating meteorological reporter to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April-June, 1881; 2nd grade of educational officers, Feb., 1890; officiating inspector of schools, July, 1890; author of 'Elementary Statics and Dynamics,' and 'The Indian Arithmetic.'

BOVILL, EDWARD, M.B., Surgeon-Major, Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service 30th Nov., 1875, and served on general, jail, and hospital duty; civil surgeon, Dec., 1885.

BOWEN, CHARLES, Colonel, R.E., Public Works Department, Madras.—First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1858; arrived in India, April, 1863; joined the Madras P. W. department, Nov., 1863, and, from June, 1864, served under the Mysore Government; executive engineer, March, 1867; assistant to chief

engineer, and secretary to chief commissioner, Mysore, June, 1869; officiating engineer in chief and joint secretary to government, Feb. to June, 1880; served in the Madras P. W. department, April, 1883, to September, 1885, as deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government; served again under the Mysore Government from Sept., 1885; superintending engineer, April, 1886.

BOWER, GEORGE, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Caistor Grammar School, Eton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 10th December, 1884; attached to the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner; officiated as under secretary to government, March, 1889; joint magistrate, March, 1890; deputy superintendent, family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, April, 1890.

BOWIE, MATHEW MORTON, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Central Provinces.—Arrived in India, 20th June, 1858; from June, 1863, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, and deputy commissioner, and was placed on special duty in connection with the Delhi assemblage, Nov., 1876; commissioner of excise, superintendent of stamps, and inspector-general of registration, Central Provinces, June, 1880; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Nagpur, Sept., 1880; inspector-general of police and prisons, Central Provinces, April, 1882; colonel, June, 1888; officiating commissioner, Sept., 1888.

BOWRING, LEWIN BENTHAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Mount Radford School, Exeter, and at Leipzig; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1843; assistant magistrate and collector, 1845; assistant to treasurer, Calcutta, 1846; assistant to resident at Lahore, 1847-49; deputy commissioner, Punjab, 1849-54; private secretary to Lord Canning, 1858-62; chief commissioner of Mysore and Coorg, 1862-70; retired, 1870, has published 'Eastern Experiences,' and contributed papers to the Asiatic Society of Bengal.

BOYD, CHARLES CLIFFORD, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Haileybury College, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1891.

BOYD, HUGH ARTHUR, M.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at Banff Grammar School, Old Aberdeen Gymnasium, Aberdeen University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 4th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

BOYS, HENRY SCOTT, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Harrow, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1863; from Dec., 1864, served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and assistant settlement officer, and was appointed superintendent of the encumbered estates of Sitapur and Kheri, June, 1872; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1881; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; 1st grade, April, 1887; acted as commissioner of division in 1883, 1887, and 1888; retired, 1889.

BRACKENBURY, HENRY, Lieut.-General, C.B., R.A.—First commission, 1st Sept., 1837; served in the Indian Mutiny, 1858, the Ashanti expedition, 1873, and the South African war, 1879-80; assistant adjutant and quartermaster-general, Cyprus, July, 1878; military secretary, South Africa, May, 1879; private secretary to Viceroy of India, March to June, 1880; military attaché, Paris, Jan., 1881; deputy adjutant and quartermaster-general, Egypt, Sept., 1884; brigadier-general, Egypt, Jan., 1885; deputy quartermaster-general at headquarters (intelligence branch), Jan., 1886; deputy adjutant-general, and director of military intelligence, June, 1887, to March, 1891, when he was appointed military member of viceroy's council; took his seat, 25th April, 1891.

BRACKENBURY, MAULE CAMPBELL, Major, R.E., Railway Department, Government of India.—First commission, June, 1865; arrived in India, June, 1875; joined the P. W. department, June, 1875; executive engineer, March, 1877; served in the Perak expedition, 1875-76 (medal and clasp), and the Afghan War, 1879-80 (medal and two clasps and bronze star); officiating deputy consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Lahore, 1881; assistant manager, Punjab northern state railway, Feb., 1883; officiating manager and traffic superintendent, Mysore railway, April, 1883; acting deputy consulting engineer, Bombay, Oct., 1884; deputy manager, North Western State Railway, Sind Section, Dec., 1885; 1st class, state railway revenue establishment, Jan., 1886; manager, Sind Section, March, 1887; under secretary to government of India, railway branch public works department, Nov., 1887, and deputy director-general of railways, April, 1888; officiating deputy secretary to the Government of India, public works department, Feb. to June, 1889; officiating manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Railway, July, 1890.

BRADBURY, JAMES FRANCIS, Bengal C.S.—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 16th Oct., 1868; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1881; also served as officiating political agent, Hill Tipperah, Sept., 1876, and officiating junior secretary, board of revenue, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Aug., 1885; 1st grade, Feb., 1890.

BRADFORD, SIR EDWARD RIDLEY COLBORNE, Colonel, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., A.D.C., I.S.C.—Cornet, 6th light cavalry, Madras, Feb., 1854; proceeded to Persia under General Jacob in 1856; political assistant in Western Malwa, and in command of 1st Regiment Central India Horse, Nov., 1860; political agent, Bhagelcund, 1871; political agent, Bhurtpur, Jan., 1873; general superintendent of operations for suppression of thuggee and dacoitee, May, 1874; agent to governor-general, Rajputana, Dec., 1878; also chief commissioner of Ajmere, secretary, political and secret department, India office, Feb., 1887; accompanied H.R.H. Prince Albert Victor on his visit to India in 1889-90; K.C.B., May, 1890; resigned on his appointment as chief commissioner metropolitan police, London, June, 1890.

BRADLEY, HERBERT, B.L., Madras C.S.—

Educated at Winchester College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in South Canara as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting head assistant, and was in charge of the South Canara forests, Sept., 1883, to April, 1885; acting deputy commissioner, salt revenue, April, 1885, and on special duty with the commissioner, July, 1885; secretary to the commissioner of salt, abkari and separate revenue, April, 1888.

BRADLEY, J. A., Legal Adviser's Department, India Office.—Clerk to the legal adviser to the secretary of state, 1877.

BRADSHAW, JOHN, M.A., LL.D., Madras Education Department.—Educated at Ennis-killen Royal School and Trinity College, Dublin; head master of Bishop Corrie's School, Madras, 1868; head master, Mangalore College, 31st Dec., 1870; acting inspector of schools, Sep., 1871; member of conference of inspectors of schools, Jan., 1873; professor of history, Presidency College, April, 1875, continuing to act as inspector of schools; fellow of University of Madras, July, 1875; from Sep., 1872, has served as inspector of schools, and was promoted to 3rd class of the department, March, 1884; member of committee for preparing a scheme for examination for the degree of licentiate of teaching, Oct.-Nov., 1883; board of examiners, Oct., 1884; member of committee for revision of the grant-in-aid code, 1885; president of committee for preparation of the pre-matriculation text, 1886 and 1888; president of Middle school examination committee, 1888; vice-chairman of board of examiners for upper secondary examination, 1889-90; has edited the Aldine editions (new series) of Gray and Milton, Chesterfield's Letters, and an edition of Milton with notes, and has also published an English Anthology, and a concordance to Milton.

BRANDIS, SIR DIETRICH, Ph.D., K.C.I.E.—Educated at Universities of Copenhagen, Gottingen, and Bonn; lecturer on botany at University of Bonn, 1849; served in the forest department of the government of India from 16th Jan., 1856, and was appointed inspector-general of forests, April, 1864; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; retired, 1883; appointed a member of the board of visitors of the Royal Indian Engineering College, 1886; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; author of 'Forest Flora of North-West and Central India, London, 1874,' of the article on 'Teak,' in the Encyclopædia Britannica, &c., &c.; is director of the practical course of forestry on the continent in connection with Cooper's Hill College.

BRANDRETH, ASHTON MILNES, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, North-Western Provinces.—First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1855; arrived in India 30th Dec., 1857; served in the public works department from 8th Jan., 1858, as assistant engineer, assistant principal, Thomason College, and executive engineer; transferred to the Punjab, Sept., 1861, and served on the Western Jumna Canal; assistant secretary, government of India, May to Dec., 1863; assistant secretary, public works department, Punjab, May, 1867; officiating assistant to chief engineer and secretary to Government, Nov., 1869; executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1871; officiating superintendent

ing engineer, Western Jumna Canal, April, 1871; principal, Thomason College, 1879 to 1891.

BRANDT, FRANCIS, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, under secretary to government in the revenue department, and in the departments under the chief secretary, acting sub-secretary to board of revenue, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; on special duty, board of revenue, 1872; acting collector of sea customs, Madras, April, 1874; collector and magistrate, South Canara, Oct., 1877, and afterwards in the Nilgiris, 1883; district and sessions judge, Trichinopoly, June, 1878; member of rent law committee, Sept., 1882; judge of the high court, Madras, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras University, March, 1886; retired, Dec., 1887.

BRANFOOT, ARTHUR MUDGE, Surgeon-Major, M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Madras Medical Department.—Assistant-surgeon, 30th March, 1872; arrived in India, 28th Dec., 1872, and served under the military department till March, 1873, and subsequently as civil surgeon at Cocanada, and Madras, and as resident surgeon at the general hospital, Madras, and professor of pathology in the Medical College, April-July, 1878; assistant physician, general hospital, and professor of hygiene, Medical College, July, 1878; superintendent of the lying-in-hospital, and professor of midwifery, Medical College, July, 1881; surgeon-major, March, 1884; fellow of Madras University, March, 1886.

BRANSON, JAMES CHARLES EMERTON, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 16th Jan., 1882, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, 3rd grade, officiating deputy-commissioner, and officiating city magistrate of Lucknow; transferred to Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1885; officiating assistant accountant-general Madras, March, 1886, and deputy accountant-general, Bombay, March, 1889.

BRANSON, JAMES HENRY SPRING, B.L., Madras Judicial Department.—Crown prosecutor and public prosecutor for town of Madras, 4th July, 1885; acting advocate general, March, 1887; additional member of Madras Legislative Council, March, 1887; advocate general, Madras, Sept., 1889.

BRASHER, ALFRED, late Indo-European Telegraph Department.—Appointed traffic manager, Persian Gulf telegraphs, 1863; superintendent, London office, 1867; director of traffic 1880; had charge of London training school in autumn, 1863; had executive charge of examinations for Indian telegraph department from 1867 to the opening of Cooper's Hill College; projected mean tariff between India and Europe, which replaced rates to different countries; has been on special duty to Constantinople, Vienna, Berlin, and Berne; has made public translations of the international telegraph conventions of Rome, St. Petersburg, London, and Berlin; retired, 1888.

BRERETON, ASHLEY F., Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Educated at Bedford School; supernumerary writer, 13th Feb., 1860; temporary clerk, Dec., 1860; clerk

in registry department, Jan., 1869; junior clerk, financial department, April, 1874; senior clerk, Nov., 1885; transferred to revenue and statistics department, 1886.

BRERETON, HERBERT JAMES, Financial Department, Government of India.—Educated at Bedford Grammar School; joined the service, 7th April, 1873; from Feb., 1874, served in the office of the accountant-general, N.W. Provinces; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, July, 1877; N.W. Provinces, Nov., 1877; Burma, April, 1878; Punjab, Aug., 1879; N.W. Provinces, March, 1884; Central Provinces, March, 1888; N.W. Provinces, July, 1888; comptroller, Assam, June, 1890.

BRERETON, REGINALD HUGH, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1889.

BRETT, ALFRED CORBYN, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial department; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, March, 1877; from May, 1878, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, Jan., 1878.

BRETT, CECIL MICHAEL WILFORD, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Guildford Grammar School, and Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1874; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, April, 1885; member central examination committee, April, 1886; registrar, high court (appellate side), April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1890; officiating judicial commissioner, Chota Nagpore, April, 1891.

BRIDGES, ALBERT, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating district judge, &c.

BRIDGES, JAMES EMILE, B.L., Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Royal College, Tournai, and University of Bonn; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1876, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, land revenue, and agricultural department, and officiating director of agriculture, Nov., 1884; deputy commissioner, 4th grade, Aug. 1886, 3rd grade, Dec., 1888; officiating chief secretary, March-July, 1890; commissioner, March, 1891.

BRIGGS, W. H., Surgeon-Major, late Madras Medical Service.—First commission, 30th Sept., 1875; surgeon-major, June, 1885; medical officer on personal staff of governor of Madras, Dec., 1886; served in the Jowaki campaign, 1877-1878 (medal and clasp), the Afghan campaigns, 1878-1880 (medal), and the Nile campaign, 1884-1885 (medal and clasps and Khedive's star).

BRIGHT, WILLIAM ROBERT, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Winchester, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1880; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1890.

BROADFOOT, RAMSAY DOUGLAS, B.L., Madras C.S.—Educated at Kensington School; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1888.

BROCKMAN, WILLIAM DRAKE, A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, North-Western Provinces.—Served in the Punjab as sub-assistant revenue surveyor, 1852–55; joined Thomason College at Roorkee, and from there was appointed to the public works department, April, 1858, and served in the N.W. Provinces in the buildings and roads branch as assistant engineer; transferred temporarily to railway department, Nov., 1859, to Dec., 1860; employed on famine relief works on Ganges Canal in 1861, and again in 1877–78 in the Rae Bareli division; executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1874; on special duty in the public works secretariat, Nov., 1878; superintendent of works, Agra, Oct., 1883; superintending engineer, 3rd class, March, 1886; superintending engineer, 1st circle, provincial works, Oct., 1887, and superintending engineer, 2nd class, Nov., 1887; 1st superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; charge of 3rd circle of superintendence, buildings and roads branch, June, 1890.

BRODIE, NORMAN SOMERVILLE, B.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at University College School and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India 12th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant to collector and magistrate; acting assistant to government, judicial and legislative departments, Dec., 1890 to April, 1891, and from July, 1891; has also acted as under secretary in the revenue department.

BRODIE, VERNOR ALEXANDER, Madras C.S.—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India 6th Jan., 1878, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, forest settlement officer, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; senior assistant to collector, magistrate, and agent, Vizagapatam, Feb., 1885; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Malabar, Jan., 1891; acting collector and magistrate, S. Kanara, Jan., 1891.

BRJENDRA COOMAR SEAL, B.L., Bengal C.S.—From 23rd Oct., 1865, served in the subordinate judicial department, Bengal, and was appointed subordinate judge, 4th grade, May, 1873, and district judge, Bankoora, and assistant session judge, Burdwan, April, 1881; district and sessions judge, June, 1887.

BRJENDRA NATH DÉ, M.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at Hare School, Calcutta, Canning College, Lucknow, Calcutta University, and St. Mary's Hall, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 6th

September, 1875; served in the revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1886.

BROOKE, THOMAS HENRY BARSTOW, Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, April, 1855; arrived in India, 13th May, 1855; was employed under the military department up to 1864, including service in the China expedition, 1857–59; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; acted as superintendent of the school of industry and central jail, and as judge of the small cause court, Jubbulpore, 1874; cantonment magistrate, Saugor, Nov., 1874; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, March, 1883; reverted to military duty, 1889.

BROOKE, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the Indian telegraph department, 13th Sept., 1857; superintendent, Bombay, Jan., 1866; served in the Punjab, 1860–1871; 1st grade superintendent, Aug., 1877, officiating as director of traffic; director, construction branch, June, 1883; officiating deputy director-general, Nov., 1886, to Feb., 1887; and Aug., 1887, to March, 1888; permanent, July, 1889; director-general of telegraphs, March, 1890.

BROOKE, WILLIAM SAURIN, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, Dec., 1858; arrived in India 3rd January, 1859; served in the Central Provinces from Jan., 1863, as assistant commissioner; acting assistant settlement officer, Chanda, Oct., 1865; acting registrar-general, Central Provinces, Jan., 1869; cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, May, 1869; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1873; acting inspector-general of police and jails, Aug.–Nov., 1883.

BROUN, JOHN ALEXANDER, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at foreign schools, University College, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1879; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner and cantonment magistrate; joint magistrate, April, 1888; officiating district judge, April, 1890; officiating magistrate and collector, April, 1891.

BROWN, CHARLES DALLAS, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Charterhouse and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

BROWN, FRANCIS DAVID MILLETT, Colonel, A.I.C.E., V.C., I.S.C., Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 8th Dec., 1855; lieutenant, 1st Bengal European regiment, June, 1857; served in the Indian mutiny and was present at siege and capture of Delhi and Lucknow (medal, 2 clasps, Victoria cross); also in the N.W. Frontier campaign, the 2nd Eusufzai expedition, and the Umbeyla campaign (medal and clasp); served in public works department, N.W. Provinces (irrigation) from Sept., 1865; executive engineer, March, 1871; assistant secretary, N.W. Provinces, Oct., 1871; superintending engineer, April, 1889; principal, Thomason Civil Engineering College, 1891.

BROWN, FREDERICK LATHAM, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant engineer, 15th July, 1869; transferred to superior accounts establishment, April, 1878; examiner, state railway accounts, Lower Burma, Nov., 1885; officiating engineer-in-chief and manager, Burma state railway, Dec., 1885, and again in Sept., 1886; examiner, telegraph accounts, March, 1889.

BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIOU GRANT, Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Burma division of the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in Burma 9th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner.

BROWN, JOHN CROMMELIN, M.A., B.L., Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India 24th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating judicial assistant; was employed in the public works department, Nov., 1879, to April, 1880; on special duty, Dec., 1880; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1887; officiating divisional judge, May, 1891.

BROWN, RICHARD CECIL, Bombay C.S.—Educated at the Clergy Orphan School, Canterbury, and Keble College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as assistant magistrate and collector.

BROWN, THOMAS EDWIN BURTON, M.D., C.I.E., Brigade-Surgeon, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department in 1858; arrived in India, 11th Feb., 1859; from Nov., 1860, served as professor of chemistry and chemical examiner, medical school, Lahore; principal, medical school, March, 1872; professor of surgery and midwifery in addition to his other duties, April-Dec., 1876; also officiated as civil surgeon of Lahore on several occasions, and as meteorological reporter, July-Aug., 1879; author of a work on the symptoms and treatment of the poisons used in the Punjab; retired Oct., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891.

BROWN, WILLIAM BARCLAY, B.A., Bengal C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Ayr Academy and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after competition of 1883; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1886; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, June, 1889.

BROWNE, CLEMENT METCALFE, Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Department.—First commission, Bombay Engineers, 1854; joined the public works department, June, 1856, as assistant engineer; transferred to Punjab, May, 1859; executive engineer, 1st grade, Sept., 1868; officiating assistant to chief engineer, Punjab, and assistant secretary, public works department, Oct., 1868; assistant to chief engineer, military works, Murree, April, 1872; officiating superintending engineer, military works, Allahabad, Aug., 1873; superintending engineer, Punjab (provincial), Feb., 1877; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Jan., 1883; rank of chief engineer, March, 1886; chief engineer and secretary, public works department, Burma, Oct., 1886; chief

engineer, 2nd class, Nov., 1886; officiating chief engineer and secretary, government of Bengal, April, 1887; retired, May, 1888.

BROWNE, LORD HENRY ULICK, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College, Dec., 1850; arrived 1st Sept., 1851; served in the revenue and judicial department, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, March, 1866; also served as private secretary to the president of the governor-general's council, Feb., 1858, under secretary to the government of Bengal, July, 1859, officiating under secretary to the government of India, home and financial department, April, 1860, registrar of the Sudder Dewanny and Nizamat Adawlut, April, 1861, superintendent of stamps and stationery, Oct., 1865, and officiating additional judge, Jessoro and Nuddea, Feb., 1867; commissioner, Chittagong division, Dec., 1869, and subsequently of the Presidency, and Rajshahye and Cooch Behar divisions; chairman of the justices of the peace, Calcutta, Feb., 1872, and officiating member, board of revenue, June, 1878; retired, Oct., 1886.

BROWNE, SIR JAMES, Maj.-Gen., K.C.S.I., C.B., R.E., Military Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Aug., 1858; joined the public works department, June, 1860, as assistant engineer in the Punjab; executive engineer, April, 1866; transferred to military works, 1873, and subsequently to the railway branch, Oct., 1874; superintendent of works, Indus Bridge, Indus Valley state railway, Jan., 1875; during 1878-79 served in the Afghan war as superintending engineer with the Quetta field force (medal); C.S.I., July, 1879; assistant secretary to government, buildings and road branch, Punjab, May, 1882; superintending engineer, 3rd class, military works, Oct., 1882; served in the Egyptian expedition as commanding engineer, 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, 3rd class Osmanieh, C.B.); transferred temporarily to railway branch for employment on the Hurnai road, Biluchistan, 1883; engineer-in-chief, Sind-Pishin state railway, 1884; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Jan., 1884, 1st class, March, 1887; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1888; quarter-master-general in India, sub pro tem., with local rank of major-general, May, 1889.

BROWNE, Ven. JAMES FRANCIS, B.D., Ecclesiastical Department, Madras.—Joined the service as junior chaplain, Madras, 20th July, 1870; senior chaplain, July, 1880; archdeacon of the diocese of Madras and commissary to the Lord Bishop, Feb., 1884; senior joint chaplain of St. George's Cathedral, Feb., 1884; in charge of the diocese, May to Oct., 1884, and again in May, 1890; fellow of Madras university, March, 1886.

BROWNE, SAMUEL HASLETT, Surgeon-Major, Punjab Medical Department.—Joined the service, March, 1874, and arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1874; served on hospital duty and on military duty in Afghanistan in 1878; from Aug., 1879, served in the Central Provinces on general and jail duty; served under the government of India from Dec., 1886, in joint medical charge, Simla; appointed principal of medical college, Lahore, Oct., 1880.

BROWNE, WILLIS, F.I.A., Funds Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 15th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; actuarial clerk, Feb., 1882; fellow of institute of actuaries, 1887; actuary, 1890.

BROWNE, WILLIAM RICHARD, M.D., Ch.M., Surgeon-Major, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, April, 1873; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1873, and served on military and civil and jail duty in Madras; resident surgeon in general hospital, and professor of pathology, Medical College, Nov., 1883; surgeon-major, April, 1885; acted for various periods as surgeon in general hospital, and professor of surgery, Medical College, and was confirmed in that appointment, May, 1890; field service in Rumpah Hill tracts and Jeypore territory in 1879-80.

BROWNING, COLIN ARROTT ROBERTSON, M.A., C.I.E., late Educational Department, Central Provinces.—Senior inspector of schools, N.W. Provinces, 26th June, 1862; acted as director of public instruction, Central Provinces, May, 1864, to March, 1865, and as inspector-gen. of education, Central Provinces, April-Oct., 1868, and again March, 1869, to July, 1870; director of public instruction in Oudh, July, 1870; inspector-general of education, Central Provinces, Oct., 1873; on special duty at Calcutta in connection with education conference in 1882; C.I.E., Jan., 1886; retired Jan., 1889; author of 'The Indian Official Thesaurus,' 'The Annals of the Indian Empire,' 'Mahomedan Education in Oude,' 'A List of Sanscrit Manuscripts existing in Oudh,' and various reports on education in the Central Provinces; has edited several school and text books, and was joint editor of the 1st edition of the Central Provinces Gazetteer.

BROWNLOW, HENRY ALEXANDER, Lieut.-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Dec., 1849; deputy superintendent, Ganges Canal, Feb., 1852; and afterwards on the Eastern and Western Jumna Canals; during the mutiny served at the battle of Budlekeserai, the siege of Delhi, and in the Rohilkund campaign (medal and clasp); superintendent, Eastern Jumna Canals, 1858; superintending engineer, N.W. Provinces, irrigation, March, 1865; 1st grade superintending engineer, Sept., 1870; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary, irrigation, N.W. Provinces, Feb., 1872; superintending engineer, Bari Doab, Punjab, April, 1874; acted as chief engineer and joint secretary, Punjab, Feb.-May, 1876, and as chief engineer and secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, irrigation, June, 1876; officiating inspector-general of irrigation and deputy secretary to government of India, April, 1877; chief engineer, 1st class, Jan., 1879; chief engineer and secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, irrigation, July, 1878, and afterwards in the buildings and roads branch; inspector-general of irrigation and deputy secretary to government of India, Feb., 1882; retired, Nov., 1886.

BROWNRIGG, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Rossall, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1881, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant settlement officer, Nov., 1888; joint magistrate, April, 1890; has acted as city magistrate, inspector-general of registration, &c., and magistrate and collector.

BRUCE, ALEXANDER BANNERMAN, B.A., Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Glasgow

Academy, Chanowty School, Aberdeen, Milne's Institution, Fochabers, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

BRUCE, HENRY ALEXANDER, M.D., Principal Inspector-General.—Educated at the University of St. Andrew's and the University of Edinburgh, where he graduated M.D. in 1829; diploma of the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh, 1828; assistant surgeon on the Bengal medical establishment, 1830; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1831; was employed on regimental duty, and served through the Cabul campaign, 1838 to 1841; was present at the storming of Ghuznee, 1839 (medal), and with the garrison of Cabul, 1839-40; in medical charge of the force sent to oppose Dost Mahomed in the Hindoo Koosh, Sept., 1840; surgeon to the envoy of Herat, Jan., 1841; was posted to the Sirmoor rifle battalion, Feb., 1841; principal medical storekeeper to the army of reserve, Nov., 1842; served in the Sutlej campaign, 1846, and was present at the battles of Aliwal (medal) and Sobraon (clasp); rank of surgeon, June, 1847; medical storekeeper, Cawnpore, and staff surgeon, Dec., 1852; superintending surgeon, Sirhind circle, May, 1859; inspector-general of hospitals, Upper Provinces, April, 1862; principal inspector-general, medical department, November, 1865; retired, Sept., 1866; Fellow of the University of Calcutta, Jan., 1865.

BRUCE, RICHARD ISAAC, C.I.E., Punjab commission.—Served in the Punjab as extra assistant-commissioner from May, 1863; assistant commissioner, Dec., 1868; prepared a gazetteer of the Dera Ghazi Khan district, and a manual of the Biluch language; on special duty in connection with Major Sandeman's 2nd Kalat mission, 1876; 1st assistant to agent to governor-general, Biluchistan, Feb., 1877; compiled a history of the Marri-Biluchi tribe; acted as agent, Biluchistan in 1878, 1882 and 1885; political agent at Thal-Chotiali from 1882; rejoined Punjab commission and appointed deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, May, 1888; 1st grade, Nov., 1889; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Derajat, July, 1890.

BUCK, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, Kt., B.L., LL.D., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Clare College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1862; from May, 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating magistrate and collector, settlement officer, and officiating secretary, board of revenue; director of agriculture and commerce, N.W. Provinces, Feb., 1875; commissioner for the government of India at the Melbourne International Exhibition of 1880; officiated as secretary to the Government of India in the revenue and agricultural department, July, 1881, and was confirmed in that appointment, April, 1882.

BUCKLAND, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A., Barrister-at-Law, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Eton and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1870; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, Dec., 1870; attaché, foreign department, Nov., 1871; officiating canton-

ment magistrate and small cause court judge, Barrackpore, Jan., 1874; on special duty in Behar, Jan., 1874; on famine duty, March, 1874; officiating junior secretary, government of Bengal, April, 1875; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, April, 1874, to Jan., 1877; assistant secretary, government of Bengal, Aug., 1876; private secretary to Sir Richard Temple during his mission to Madras and Bombay, Jan., 1877; private secretary to governor of Bombay, May, 1877; officiating under-secretary, government of India, home department, May, 1878; private secretary to president of governor-general's council, Nov., 1878; officiating press commissioner with government of India, Feb., 1879; superintendent of stamps and stationery, Bengal, May, 1880; officiating junior secretary, board of revenue, Oct., 1882, and again in 1883, 1884, and 1886; confirmed, May, 1887; officiating secretary to the Government of Bengal, general revenue and statistical departments, July, 1889, again in Oct., 1889, and from March, 1891; secretary to Board of Revenue, May, 1890; edited 'Excise Manual,' 1884.

BUCKLE, HENRY, Judicial Department, Burma.—Served in the Burma commission from 7th Aug., 1867, as assistant commissioner, and was in charge of the town magistrate's office, Akyab, Sept., 1871, to Nov., 1874, and of the Hill Tracts of Northern Arakan, Dec., 1874, to Jan., 1876; in command of an expedition beyond the frontier against the Shandus, Nov., 1876; from June, 1878, served as deputy commissioner in various districts; president, Henzada municipal committee, Dec., 1884; officiating commissioner, Arakan division, March, 1888, and of the eastern division, March, 1890; additional sessions judge, Jan., 1891.

BUCKLEY, LLEWELLYN EDDISON, Madras C.S.—Educated at Clifton College, and at New College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in Madras, 19th Sept., 1887, and served in the revenue survey department, and as assistant to magistrate and collector.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK D'OLBERT, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Kingstown Grammar School, St. Aubin's Grammar School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 11th Jan., 1865, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; judge, small cause court, Delhi, Jan., 1873; judicial assistant, Dec., 1877; district judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Oct., 1885; and 2nd grade, Nov., 1889.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK SHORE, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, officiating cantonment magistrate, assistant superintendent of Dehra Dún, small cause court judge, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; joint magistrate, 1st grade, June, 1885; magistrate and collector, April, 1889.

BULLOCK, ROBERT, Colonel, I.S.C., late Berar Commission.—First commission Madras army (infantry), 11th Dec., 1858; from July, 1862, served in the Berar commission as assistant

commissioner, officiating assistant resident, officiating small cause court judge, and deputy commissioner (March, 1880); boundary commissioner for demarcation of the Dhangs frontier, Feb. to June, 1886; judicial commissioner, sub pro tem., Aug. 1888; civil and sessions judge, July, 1889; retired, April, 1891.

BULMAN, ALBERT ROBINSON, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Richmond Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; officiating secretary to financial commissioner, July, 1874; judicial assistant, 1st grade, March, 1878; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Dec., 1882; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Lahore, Aug., 1884; 1st grade deputy commissioner, March, 1886; officiating commissioner, Umballa, Jan., 1887; retired, April, 1889.

BUNBURY, CECIL EDWARD FRANCIS, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Wellington College and Keble College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1885, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

BURGESS, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.S.I., Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1870, and served in the British Burma commission as assistant commissioner, assistant collector of customs, and deputy commissioner; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, and officer in charge of state prisoners, Jan., 1879; secretary to chief commissioner, July, 1881; officiating commissioner, Sept., 1884; commissioner, April, 1886; C.S.I., 1st Jan., 1889; judicial commissioner, Upper Burma, Dec., 1891.

BURGESS, JAMES, C.I.E., LL.D., F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S., M. Soc. As., Paris, late Archaeological Survey of India.—Was educated at Dumfries, Glasgow, and Edinburgh; archaeological surveyor and reporter for Western India, 15th Jan., 1874; also archaeological surveyor for Southern India, Nov. 1881; director general of the archaeological survey of India, March, 1886; was a fellow of the University of Bombay; retired 1890; author of scientific papers in the Philosophical Magazine, 'The Indian Antiquary,' 'Views of Architecture and Scenery in Gujerat and Rajputana,' 'Reports of the Archaeological Survey of Western and Southern India,' and, conjointly with the late J. Ferguson, LL.D., C.I.E., 'The Rock Temples of India.'

BURKE, John, M.I.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, 2nd June, 1864; superintendent, 2nd grade, Aug. 1883; officiating director of traffic, Feb., 1886; superintendent, Class IV., 2nd grade, Oct., 1887; has held charge of various divisions.

BURKITT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., B.L., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; called to the bar at the Middle Temple, appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 11th Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector,

1st grade, March, 1875, and magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Sept., 1880; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; additional judicial commissioner in Oudh, March, 1891.

BURLS, E. GRANT, Store Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, India office, 20th Oct., 1863; junior clerk, store department, Dec., 1865; senior clerk, Jan., 1875; assistant director-general of stores, June, 1891.

BURLTON, HENRY MONTGOMERY BARTON, Colonel, I.S.C.—Ensign, 5th Native Infantry, Bengal, Aug., 1855; served in the Indian Mutiny, 1858-59, and was present at the capture of Gwalior, the actions of Jaora and Alipore, and other operations (medal and clasp); served subsequently in the Bhutan campaign, 1862, and the Abyssinian expedition, 1867-68 (medal); assistant cantonment magistrate, Morar, 1870; cantonment magistrate, Mhow, 1881; cantonment magistrate, Morar, Dec., 1884, and at Umballa, Nov., 1886; cantonment magistrate, 1st class, March, 1887; reverted to military duty, 1891.

BURN, CHARLES WILLIAM, Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India 26th Nov., 1889, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

BURNE, HENRY KNIGHTLEY, C.B., General, I.S.C.—Ensign, Dec., 1843; arrived in India, 25th May, 1844; served throughout the Sutlej campaign, 1845-46, including battles of Mood-kee and Ferozeshah, and with the expedition against Kota Kangra (medal and clasp); during the Burmese war of 1852-53, was military secretary to General Godwin, commander of the force, and was present at the capture of Prome and Pegu (medal and clasp and brevet rank of major); acted as deputy assistant quartermaster-general, May-Oct. 1849; and as cantonment joint magistrate, Umballa, Nov., 1849; acting brigade major, Umballa, 1849-51, and as deputy assistant adjutant-general, Sirhind division, 1851-52; deputy judge advocate general, April, 1852; and assistant secretary to government of India, military department, July, 1856; 1st assistant secretary, Jan., 1858; deputy secretary, March, 1862; secretary to government of India, military department, June, 1870 to Dec., 1881; C.B., May, 1875.

BURNE, KNIGHTLEY GREY, Burma Commission.—Served in the Bengal police as assistant superintendent and acting district superintendent, from 26th Oct., 1865 to May 1872, and from the latter date in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner, and acted as town magistrate and superintendent of jail at Moulmein, 1872-75; officiating director of public instruction, Aug.-Nov., 1882; deputy commissioner, July, 1886.

BURNE, SIR OWEN TUDOR, Major-Gen., K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Council of India.—Ensign, 20th foot, 15th April, 1855; served in the Crimea; also in the Indian Mutiny of 1858-59, and was present in fifteen actions (including the siege and capture of Lucknow); promoted for gallantry at Lucknow to captaincy and brevet majority. After serving in the field as adjutant of regiment, and brigade-major to a field-column, was appointed military secretary to the commander-in-chief in India (the late Field Marshal Lord Strathnairn), 1861-62, and afterwards

A.D.C., and at times officiating military secretary to that general officer when commander of the forces in Ireland, 1865-68; private secretary to viceroy of India (Earl of Mayo), 1869-72; political A.D.C. to secretary of state for India, 1872; assistant secretary in political and secret department, India Office, 1873; secretary in that department, 1874; on special duty in India as private secretary to the viceroy (Earl of Lytton), 1876-78; member of the council of India, 1st Jan., 1887; was a royal commissioner of the Health and the Colonial and Indian exhibitions of 1882 and 1886; vice-president of the Society of Arts, 1888-92; appointed by H. M. the Queen a governor of the Imperial Institute, 1891; is a royal commissioner for the World's Exposition in Chicago, 1891-93; vice-president of the council of India, 1891-92; author of 'Clyde and Strathnairn' in the 'Rulers of India' series, and of various contributions to periodical literature.

BURRIDGE, CHARLES E., Store Department, India Office.—Appointed 2nd class clerk, 16th March, 1888.

BURROWS, LEONARD ROBERT, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Lincoln Diocesan School, St. Nicholas College, Lancing, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; assistant collector and magistrate, Dec., 1865; president of municipal commission, Madras, 1875-1880; sub-collector and joint magistrate, March, 1882; acting collector of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, Sept., 1883; collector and magistrate and additional sessions judge, Nilgiris, Sept., 1884; retired, Aug., 1889.

BURTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Colonel, R.E., late Madras P.W. Department.—Arrived in India, 26th July, 1857, and was appointed to the public works department, May, 1858, and served in Oudh and Hyderabad; executive engineer, July, 1865; exchanged for temporary service out of India, and appointed to Portsmouth district, and charge of Needles division, Aug., 1867; proceeded to China, Nov., 1875, where he acted for some time, in 1876-7, as commanding Royal engineers; returned to India, Nov., 1877; member of defence committee on coast defences, Dec., 1877; executive engineer, Madras, Jan., 1878; superintending engineer, 3rd class, April, 1889; retired, Dec., 1890.

BUSTEED, HENRY ELMSLEY, M.D., C.I.E., Brigade Surgeon, late Mint Department, Calcutta.—First commission, Indian medical service (Madras establishment), Aug., 1855; arrived in India, March, 1856; civil surgeon, Cuddalore, Jan., 1861; assistant assay master, Madras mint, Feb., 1865; acted as assay master for various periods, and also as mint master and commissioner of paper currency; deputy assay master, Bombay, April, 1868; from Feb., 1869, served in the Calcutta mint as officiating assay master, and was appointed deputy assay master, Nov., 1870, and confirmed as assay master, Sept., 1872; also acted as mint master in 1873 and 1875; retired June, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1887.

BUTLER, JAMES, Major, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Ensign, 19th foot, Feb., 1866; arrived in Madras, 24th May, 1866; served in the Burma commission from Oct., 1868, as assistant commissioner and town magistrate; appointed a

deputy commissioner, July, 1881; president of the Henzada Municipal Committee, Oct., 1884; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Aug., 1886, and 1st grade, Dec., 1888; author of 'Mergui Gazette.'

BUTLER, SPENCER HARCOURT, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Harrow and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant collector and magistrate.

BUTLER, WILLIAM G., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Bradford and Eton; junior clerk, store department, India office, 31st Jan., 1867; assistant private secretary and précis writer to secretary of state, 1874-78; senior clerk, Dec., 1880.

BUTTERWORTH, ALAN, Madras C.S.—Educated at Elizabeth College, Guernsey, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras Civil Service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived 15th Dec., 1885, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; has acted as assistant secretary and under secretary to government.

BUTTS, HENRY HILL, Barrister-at-Law, Oudh Commission.—Extra assistant commissioner, 3rd grade, Gonda, 3rd June, 1863; from Nov., 1865, to Oct., 1873, served as assistant settlement officer and officiating settlement officer, and from the latter date as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; subordinate judge, 1st grade, Fyzabad, Aug., 1879; district judge, 3rd grade, Fyzabad, Jan., 1882; deputy commissioner, Jan. 1882; author of the 'Lucknow Settlement Report.'

BUYERS, JOHN WALKER, C.I.E., M.A., M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed executive engineer, N.W. Provinces, 18th Nov., 1869; 1st executive engineer, Jan., 1880; engineer in chief, Garo Hills survey, Dec., 1881; superintending engineer, March, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; engineer-in-chief, Mu-valley railway, July, 1889; retired, 1891.

BYATT, J., Store Department, India Office.—Assistant clerk, education department, May, 1869, to May, 1873; clerk, and class, India store depot, May, 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, July, 1888.

BYRNE, EDWARD SIMPSON, Financial Department, Government of India.—Head assistant to auditor-general of India, June, 1862; served in Hyderabad from Jan., 1865, to March, 1871, as deputy auditor and accountant-general, and was ordered on special duty to Calcutta in connection with Berar finance, Dec., 1867; from March, 1871, to Feb., 1874, served in the Punjab as deputy accountant-general and acting accountant-general, and trustee, Punjab University; served subsequently as deputy accountant-general in Assam, Feb., 1874, to Jan., 1879, and in Bombay 1879 to July, 1882, and acted as accountant-general and commissioner of paper currency, Bombay, in 1879; from July, 1882, served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as deputy accountant-general, and acted as accountant-general and deputy commissioner of paper currency, Allahabad, Sept.-Oct., 1882; deputy comptroller-general (afterwards designated deputy auditor-general) in 1st class of the financial department, July, 1883; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Sept.-Oct., 1885; deputy auditor-general, Oct.,

1885; officiated as accountant-general, Punjab, in 1889, and as deputy comptroller-general, in 1890.

CADELL, ALAN, B.A., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, Guetersloh Gymnasium, Westphalia, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; from July, 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from April, 1867, to March, 1881, served in the settlement department in Allahabad, Mozaffarnagar, and Banda, and was placed on special duty, June, 1867, and again in March, 1869, to conduct the prosecution of the Munsif of Deoband; promoted to settlement officer, 1st grade, Nov., 1879; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, May, 1880; officiating commissioner of excise and stamps and inspector-general of registration, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1883; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, March, 1885; officiating civil and sessions judge, April, 1886; commissioner, Agra division, May, 1889; Rohilkund division, Feb., 1890.

CADELL, ALAN, Alexander, Lieutenant-General, late R.E.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, 13th June, 1845; joined the public works department, April, 1848; executive engineer, Rawul Pindi, 1850; executive engineer, Nagpore, April, 1856; executive engineer in Oude road department, Dec., 1858; superintendent of works, northern road, and Saugor division, 1863; superintending engineer, Punjab, 1864; from 1867 to 1878 served in Indore, first as superintending engineer, and then as chief engineer, and secretary to the governor-general's agent for Central India; also officiated as chief engineer and secretary to government N.W. Provinces, 1874-75; chief engineer, 1st grade, Jan., 1879; chief engineer and secretary to government of Bengal, 1879-80; deputy inspector-general of military works, 1881; inspector-general, military works, 1881-82; retired, March, 1882.

CADELL, THOMAS, Colonel, V.C., I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, April, 1854; arrived in India, July, 1854; during the mutiny served at the siege of Delhi (medal, clasp, and Victoria cross); and subsequently in the operations in Oudh and Bundelcund; 2nd in command, Malwa Bheel corps, deputy Bheel agent, and political assistant, April, 1861; officiating commandant, Malwa Bheel corps, and Bheel agent, March, 1869; political agent, Eastern States, Rajputana, April, 1870; political agent, Ulwar, Nov., 1870; political agent, Marwar, Nov., 1877, and in Meywar, April, 1878; commandant, Erinpura force, and political agent, Western Rajputana, Oct., 1879; chief commissioner and superintendent, Port Blair and the Nicobars, from Dec., 1879.

CALL, C. F., Lieutenant-Colonel, R.E., late Store Department, India Office.—Entered the royal engineers, 16th July, 1866; served on the Indian establishment with the Bengal sappers and miners, and afterwards in the public works department, buildings and roads and railway branches, Feb., 1869, to Sept., 1881; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80, including the march from Kabul to Kandahar (medal and 2 clasps, bronze star, and brevet of major); assistant superintendent, India store depot, Jan., 1883, to Feb., 1887.

CALLAGHAN, T., Registry Department, India Office.—2nd class clerk, 27th March, 1885.

CALNAN, DENIS, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at French College, Blackrock, Ireland, and Queen's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant commissioner; assistant magistrate and collector, July, 1890.

CAMERON, ARCHIBALD, Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, M.D., Indian Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, April, 1868; arrived in India, Nov., 1868, and served as civil medical officer at various stations in Oudh; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Fyzabad, Dec., 1870; served subsequently in the same capacity at Bara Banki, Lucknow, Sultanpur, and other places; surgeon-major, April, 1880; in medical charge of camp of lieutenant-governor, Nov., 1884; civil surgeon, 1st class, Allahabad, Feb., 1886.

CAMERON, CHARLES, Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, Indian Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 27th July, 1859; arrived in India, 28th Jan., 1860; was employed under the military department 1860-66, and served in the Bhoontan campaign (medal); from April, 1866, served in the civil department in Oudh, as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at Bahraich and Rae Bareli; rank of surgeon, July, 1871; surgeon-major, Sept., 1875; served also as civil surgeon and jail superintendent at Bara Banki and at Gonda.

CAMERON, DUNCAN, M.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at Aberdeen University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1876; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; confirmed as joint magistrate and deputy collector, Aug., 1887; has also acted as divisional judge (Punjab), and district and sessions judge.

CAMERON, LEWIS, Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, M.D., Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1866; from Feb., 1873, served as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon in Bengal.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER WILLIAM DENNISTOWN, Captain, I.S.C.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1871; served under the military department to April, 1882, when he was appointed cantonment magistrate, N.W. Provinces, 2nd class; 1st class, March, 1891.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD COLIN, late Assam Commission.—Served in Assam from 27th April, 1861, as assistant commissioner and personal assistant to chief commissioner; was personal assistant to the chief civil officer, Bhutan field force, 1865 (medal and clasp); superintendent of emigration and embarkation agent, Dhubri, Sept., 1875; promoted to deputy commissioner, Dec., 1878; 1st grade, deputy commissioner, May, 1886; acted as commissioner, Assam Valley Districts, in 1889; retired, 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD EDWARDES, Colonel, I.S.C., late Assam Commission.—First commission, Jan., 1851; served in the Sonthal campaign, 1855-56, and in Central India, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); from May, 1862 served in the Assam

commission and was promoted to deputy commissioner, Dec., 1866; inspector of labourers, Sibsagar, Feb., 1882; 1st grade, deputy commissioner, Aug., 1883; retired, 1890.

CAMPBELL, COLIN G., India Office.—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; junior clerk, military department, 13th Jan., 1876; private secretary to permanent under secretary of state from 1877; senior clerk, 1889; appointed reading clerk to the council, 1890.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN JOHN ALFRED, Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Tonbridge School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1877, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate; transferred to Burma, Aug., 1878, and served as assistant commissioner, boundary officer, and officiating deputy commissioner; cantonment magistrate, and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, in 1883; appointed to special duty in Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Aug., 1886; deputy commissioner, May, 1888.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN McNEIL, Major, R.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Arrived, 8th April, 1869, and served in the Madras Presidency as assistant engineer and executive engineer; under-secretary to government in the public works department, Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1886; superintending engineer, July, 1890.

CAMPBELL, FRANCIS JOHNSTONE GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 20th January, 1864; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Aug., 1875, and district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, April, 1880; 1st grade, April, 1887; retired Oct., 1890.

CAMPBELL, SIR GEORGE, K.C.S.I, D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at New Academy, Edinburgh, and St. Andrew's; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College 1842; served in Rohilkund, 1843 to 1846, in usual grades of the revenue and judicial departments, and was in charge of several districts and political divisions of cis-Sutlej states, 1846-51; on furlough 1851 to 1854, during which he was associate of the Court of Queen's Bench, and published 'Modern India,' and 'India as it may be'; magistrate and collector, Azimghur, 1854; commissioner of customs, N.W. Provinces, 1855; commissioner of the cis-Sutlej states, 1855-1857; took an active part in the events following the mutiny, and was present at several engagements; personal assistant to governor-general, Lord Canning, 1857-58; judicial and financial commissioner of Oudh, 1858; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1862-66; president of the commission to enquire into the famine in Orissa, 1866-67; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, 1867-68; while on furlough, 1868-70, published a work on Irish land tenure; D.C.L., University of Oxford; lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 1871-74; retired 1874; member of council of secretary of state for India, 1874-75; resigned on election as M.P. for Kirkcaldy burghs in 1875; presided over economy and trade department at the Social Science Congress at Glasgow, 1874; re-elected for the Kirkcaldy burghs in 1880, and again in 1885; is the author of numerous statistical,

ethnological, and linguistic works on India, also of 'A Handy Book of the Eastern Question,' 1876, and of various publications on different countries visited by him.

CAMPBELL, GERALD EDWARD LYON, Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 11th Dec., 1883; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1884; head assistant, Dec., 1890.

CAMPBELL, JAMES McNABB, M.A., LL.D., C.I.E., Bombay C.S.—Educated at Glasgow Academy and Glasgow University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant conservator of forests, acting under secretary to government in the political, judicial, and educational departments, and compiler of the 'Gazetteer' and 'General Administration Report of the Bombay Presidency'; on famine duty, April–Aug., 1877; acting municipal commissioner, Bombay, April, 1880; acting under secretary to government in the revenue, financial, and general departments, Oct., 1880; acted as collector of Bombay and superintendent of stamps and stationery in 1881; 1st assistant collector, Bombay presidency, May, 1885; junior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1890.

CAMPBELL, JOHN SCARLETT, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at the Grange, Sunderland; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1847; arrived in India, Jan., 1848, and served as assistant magistrate and collector in the N.W. Provinces; joint magistrate, Moradabad, 1856; during the mutiny was present at the siege of Delhi, and subsequently took charge of the Rohtuk district as deputy commissioner, 1st class; commissioner, Saugor division, Central Provinces, Jan., 1862; judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, 1864; officiating chief commissioner, Central Provinces, 1865; judge of the chief court, Punjab, 1871; on assassination of Lord Mayo in 1872, was deputed to enquire into and report on the administration of the convict settlement in the Andaman Islands; retired, 1878; author of a 'Memo. on proposed changes in the mode of selection and training of candidates for the Indian Civil Service.'

CAMPBELL, JOHN STRATHEDEN, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1883, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; senior assistant commissioner, Garhwal, Dec., 1886; joint magistrate, Nov., 1890.

CAMPBELL, LORNE AUGUSTUS, Madras C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 30th Dec., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1866; under secretary to government of Madras, revenue department, Jan., 1875, to Feb., 1881, and acted on several occasions as secretary to Government, and as sub-secretary, board of revenue; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Oct., 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1884.

CAMPBELL, RICHARD HAMILTON, Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras Civil Service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1885; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1886.
