JAMES, M.P.—Born in 1820; educated at University College, London, where he took the degree of LL.B.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849; was returned to parliament for Halifax, April, 1859; appointed a lord of the admiralty in April, 1863; resigned in April, 1864; under-secretary of state for India, Feb. to June, 1866; third lord of the treasury, Dec., 1868; joint secretary to the treasury, Oct., 1869; president of the poor law board, March, 1871; was first president of the new local government board, Aug., 1871, to Jan., 1874, and again March–Aug., 1886.

STANTIALL, W., Public Works Department, India Office.—Educated at the City of London School; clerk, 2nd class, India Office, 25th Sept., 1882.

STANTON, FREDERICK SMITH, Major-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—Date of commission, 10th Dec., 1850; arrived in India, June, 1853; assistant engineer, public works department, Bengal, Nov., 1853; executive engineer, grand trunk road, July, 1857; deputy consulting engineer, railway department, Jan., 1860; executive engineer, 1st grade, Dec., 1863; was employed on special duties in 1863–64, and also as executive engineer in the circular and eastern canal, and Darjeeling divisions, and served under the military department with the Dooar field force; officiating consulting engineer and joint secretary in the railway branch, government of Bengal, Aug., 1867, to March, 1868, when he was transferred to Oudh in the same capacity; deputy consulting engineer and assistant secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, Oct., 1869; superintending engineer on the Rajputana state railway, Feb., 1871; temporarily

in charge of Tirhoot famine line, Feb., 1874; superintending engineer, 1st grade, April, 1874; extra deputy consulting engineer to government of India for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, Oct., 1875; engineer-in-chief, Tirhoot state railway, Feb., 1876; officiating director of state railways, north-eastern system, and officiating joint secretary to government of Bengal, May, 1877; chief engineer, 3rd class, Jan., 1880; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, P.W. department, Feb., 1880, to March, 1881; director-general of railways and deputy secretary to government of India, Feb., 1882, to Feb., 1887; chief engineer, 1st class, Feb., 1887; retired, Feb., 1887.

STAVELEY, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM DUNBAR, General, G.C.B.—Educated at the Scottish Naval and Military Academy, Edinburgh; 2nd lieutenant, 87th Foot, March, 1835; assistant military secretary to Col. W. Staveley, in Mauritius, Feb., Nov., 1842, and at Hong Kong, June, 1847, to Dec., 1850; A.D.C. to Lieutenant-General Earl Cathcart, in Canada, March, 1845, to May, 1847; served the Eastern campaign of 1854-55, including the battles of Alma and Balaklava, and the siege of Sebastopol (medal, C.B., Sardinian and Turkish medals, and 5th class of the Medjidie); brigadier-general in North China, 1860-1862 (medal); from Feb., 1862, to June, 1863, was in command of the troops during the operations against the Taepings, in the vicinity of Shanghai, resulting in the capture of the entrenched camp of Onkatz, and the towns of Kadin, Tsinpoo, Cholin, Najow, and Tserpoo; K.C.B., March, 1865; from March, 1865, to Sept., 1867, served as brigadier-general in the Bombay presidency, and was in command of the northern division and the Poona brigade successively; was second in command in the Abyssinian expedition, 1867-68, and was present at the action of Arrogee, and capture of Magdala; received the thanks of parliament, and was promoted to a major-general, 1868 (medal); major-general on the staff, western division, Devonport, Jan.-March, 1869; commander-in-chief, Bombay, and member of the governor's council, Oct., 1874, to Oct., 1878; G.C.B., 24th May, 1884.

STEAD, VEN. SAMUEL, M.A. (Oxon), late Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, March, 1863; arrived in India, 24th April, 1863, and served as chaplain at Karachi, Sholapur, cantonment of Belgaum, and Poona; senior chaplain, April, 1873; archdeacon and commissary, Nov., 1878; also held charge of the diocese of Bombay, Oct., 1877, to Nov., 1878, and Oct., 1883, to June, 1884; retired, June, 1886.

STEANE, W.C., Registry Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 9th Nov., 1860; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

STAVENSON, JOSEPH, B.A., Judicial Department, Madras.—Served from 25th Feb., 1878, as acting registrar of the high court (original side), Madras, and was confirmed in this appointment, Feb., 1880.

STEDMAN, EDWARD, C.B., Brigadier-General, I.S.C., Burma Police.—1st commission, 19th Dec., 1860; major, Dec., 1880, and lieutenant-colonel, Dec., 1886; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp), and in the Afghan war of 1878-80; with Sir Frederick Roberts in the march to Candahar; and present at the battle of Candahar (medal with clasp and bronze decoration); served in the Burma expedition of 1886-7, and com-

manded the operations in the Shan states; inspector-general of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; and of Burma, Oct., 1888.

STEEDMAN, EDWARD BRITTAIN, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Shrewsbury; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; officiating director of settlements and revenue records, March, 1887; confirmed, May, 1887; designation changed to director of land records and agriculture; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Dec., 1889; 3rd grade, Dec., 1890; author of 'Report on the Settlement of the Jhang District.'

STEEL, CHARLES DENTON, B.A., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Wesleyan Connexional School and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Oct., 1886; assistant superintendent and judge, small cause court, Dehra Dún, in 1888-9; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1889; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; on special duty, May, 1891; author of 'Allahabad Gazetteer.'

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Caius College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; services placed temporarily at disposal of public works department in connection with Punjab northern state railway, July, 1874; judicial assistant, 1st grade, Dec., 1878; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; 1st grade, Jan., 1887; retired, May, 1889.

STEEL, JOHN PHILIP, Colonel, R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Dec., 1855; joined the public works department, March, 1860, as assistant engineer, Bengal; executive engineer, April, 1868; officiating assistant to chief engineer, and assistant secretary to government of Bengal, July, 1871; officiating under secretary, government of India, public works department, March, 1873, to 1875, and again Feb., 1877; superintending engineer and secretary to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, May, 1879; superintending engineer, 1st class, May, 1886; officiating chief engineer and secretary to government, Punjab, March, 1887; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Oct., 1887; chief engineer and joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1888; 3rd chief engineer, April, 1888; officiated as secretary to government, Feb.-May, 1889; and again Nov., 1889; confirmed Dec., 1889; 2nd chief engineer, Dec., 1889.

STEINBELT, JEAN MARI CONRAD, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy

commissioner; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Mirzapur, Sept., 1878; from April, 1881, served as civil and sessions judge in Banda, and afterwards at Azamgarh; retired, April, 1887.

STEINBERG, ALFRED FREDERICK, Bengal C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Dulwich College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1881, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner, and assistant inspector of labourers.

STEPHEN, ARTHUR, M.A., M.B., Surgeon-Lieut-Colonel, Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1867; served under the military department in the Punjab as assistant surgeon and surgeon; Lahore medical school, 1874; superintendent of jail, Lahore, 1876; surgeon-major, Sept., 1879; statistical officer to government of India, sanitary and medical department, 1880; officiated as sanitary commissioner, Punjab, in 1885 and 1886, and was confirmed, Nov., 1886.

STEPHEN, CARR, Barrister-at-Law.—Served in the Punjab from 11th June, 1856, as extra assistant commissioner and small cause court judge; extra judicial assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oct., 1875; officiating divisional judge, Nov., 1885, to Jan., 1886; has published 'Indian Registration Act,' 4th edition, 'Handbook for Delhi,' and 'Archæology of Delhi.'

STEPHEN, SIR JAMES FITZJAMES, K.C.S.I., M.A., an Honorary Fellow of Trinity College, Cambridge, D.C.L., Oxford, LL.D., Edinburgh, and LL.B., London.—Educated at Eton, King's College, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge, where he graduated B.A. in 1852; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Jan., 1854; went the Midland Circuit, and became recorder of Newark-on-Trent (1859-69); legal member of the council of the governor-general of India, Dec., 1869 to 1872; appointed to the Inns of Court professor of common law, Dec., 1875, and a member of the councils of legal education and law reporting; K.C.S.I., 1877; a member of the royal commission appointed to enquire into the provisions of a draft code relating to indictable offences, 1878; judge of the high court of Justice (Queen's bench division), Jan., 1879 to 1891; president of the ordnance inquiry commission, 1886-7; has published 'General View of the Criminal Law of England,' 1863; 'Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity,' 1873; 'History of the Criminal Law,' 3 vols., 1883; 'The Digest of the Law of Evidence'; 'Digest of the Criminal Law,' 4th Edition, 1887.

STERNDALE, R. A., late Financial Department, Government of India.—Served during the mutiny as a volunteer, 1857, and took part in the operations in Central India (medal); lieutenant, Behar station guards, Jan., 1858; from Aug., 1859, served in the Central Provinces as deputy collector and assistant settlement officer; from June, 1864, served in the financial department in Nagpore, Calcutta and the Punjab, as assistant commissioner of paper currency, acting assistant accountant-general, acting deputy accountant-general, and acting assistant comptroller-general; 3rd class of financial department (new scheme), Aug., 1880; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Jan., 1884, and Madras, Nov., 1887,

retired, 1890; is a fellow of the Royal Geographical and Zoological Societies; author of 'Mammalia of British India and Ceylon,' 'Denizens of the Jungles,' 'Seoni; or Camp Life on the Satpura Range,' a 'Topographical and Historical Account of the District of Seoni,' 'Turkey and India; or Our Indian Moslems,' 'The Afghan Knife,' and other works; joint editor of the 'Journal of the Bombay Natural History Society.'

STEVENS, CHARLES CECIL, B.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at University of Melbourne; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1862; from Jan., 1863, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Oct., 1879; officiating secretary, board of revenue, April, 1882; commissioner, Chota Nagpur division, Aug., 1885; officiating chief secretary to government of Bengal, May-Nov., 1890.

STEVENS, JOHN FOSTER, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Birmingham Grammar School, at Versailles, and at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1864; arrived, 16th Oct., 1865; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, March, 1879; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Sept., 1884; 1st grade, Nov., 1888.

STEVENSON, GEORGE, Bengal C.S.—Educated at King's College, Aberdeen, and Glasgow University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; transferred to Assam, Jan., 1880, and served as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Sylhet, Jan., 1885, 2nd grade, May, 1886; returned to Bengal, Jan., 1889; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, June, 1889; magistrate and collector, 3rd grade, July, 1889; 2nd grade, July, 1890.

STEVENSON - MOORE, CHARLES JAMES, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Felsted School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

STEWART, ARTHUR BENNET, B.A., Barrister-at-Law, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Rugby and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 5th Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant political agent, acting junior collector and magistrate, and acting forest settlement officer; second assistant collector, Dec., 1882; acting postmaster-general, Bombay, Aug., 1884, to May, 1885; acting assistant judge, Poona, and assistant agent for Sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1885, judicial assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1887; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, May, 1891.

STEWART, SIR DONALD MARTIN, Bart., General, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Ensign, Bengal army (infantry), 12th Oct., 1840; adjutant,

9th Bengal native infantry, 1845; interpreter and quartermaster, 9th N.I., 1852; served against the hill tribes on the Peshawar frontier in 1854-5, and against the Bussee Kheyl tribes in 1855 (medal and clasp); commanded volunteers in Allyghur district, May and June, 1857; deputy assistant adjutant-general, Delhi field force, June, 1857; assistant adjutant-general of army, Dec., 1857; was present at siege and capture of Lucknow, and the operations in Rohilkund (medal with two clasps); deputy adjutant-general in India, Feb., 1863; brigadier-general, Abyssinian expeditionary force, 1868 (medal and C.B.); in command of Peshawar district, July, 1869; chief commissioner of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, 1871; in command of Lahore Division, April, 1876; commanding Kandahar and Southern Afghanistan force, 1878-80; commanding at Cabul and in Northern Afghanistan, 1880, till withdrawal from that country; received the thanks of both houses of parliament (K.C.B., G.C.B., baronet, and medal with clasp); military member of governor-general's council, 1880; commander-in-chief in India, and extraordinary member of the governor-general's council, 1881; member of council of India, 16th Dec., 1885.

STEWART, ROBERT BARTON, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Dulwich and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1887.

STEWART, THEODORE HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Kensington Grammar School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862, and served as assistant collector and magistrate and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty to inquire into land alienations in the Panch Mahāls, Sept., 1865; boundary settlement officer, May, 1866; on special duty to inquire into titles of land in Surat, Aug., 1867; junior collector and magistrate and political agent, Sholapur, Oct., 1879; survey and settlement commissioner, June, 1881; on special duty as member of the public service commission, Dec., 1886; 2nd grade commissioner, May, 1889; retired, April, 1891.

STEWART-WILSON, CHARLES, B.A., B.L., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Edinburgh High School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1886; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Dec., 1890; officiating under-secretary to government from Oct., 1890.

STIFFE, A.W., Captain, late I.M.—From 24th Feb., 1849, served in the Indian marine as midshipman, lieutenant, and senior lieutenant, and assistant surveyor; on special survey for telegraph cable in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1862; on special duty in Turkish Arabia, Feb., 1863; engineer, Persian Gulf telegraphs, and commanding 'Amberwithch,' Oct., 1863; officiating director, Persian Gulf telegraphs, April-Aug., 1866, and Sept., 1869, to Jan., 1871; also electrician, Aug., 1871; on special duty in England, May, 1875, and May, 1878; in command of cable steamer 'Patrick Stewart,' May-July,

1879; port officer, Calcutta, Aug., 1879; officiating superintendent of marine, July, 1880; ex officio shipping master of Calcutta, Feb., 1882; was also president of board of examiners in seamanship, registrar of shipping and wrecks, and presidency magistrate; retired, 1888; has brought out new charts and pilots of the Persian gulf and Mekran coast; also a new chart of Maskat, and a description of the Hooghly.

STIMPSON, E. J., Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st May, 1873; resigned, 24th June, 1879; reappointed, 10th July, 1880.

STOGDON, ABRAHAM WILLIAM, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Uppingham Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; judicial assistant, Sept., 1877; services placed temporarily at disposal of government of India, military department, Feb., 1881; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1883; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; 2nd grade, March, 1889; and 1st grade, Nov., 1889; officiating judge of the chief court in 1889, 1890, and from March, 1891.

STOKER, THOMAS, B.A., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Rathmines School and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; deputy superintendent of family domains of Mahārāja of Benares, March, 1878; 1st assistant secretary to government, April, 1884; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, May, 1884; officiated as secretary to government in the financial department in 1885 and 1886; settlement officer, Bulandshahr, Sept., 1886; inspector-general of registration, and commissioner of excise and stamps, Nov., 1891.

STOKES, GABRIEL, B.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Kilkenny College, Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay till Dec., 1872, and afterwards in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting principal assistant, and acting collector and magistrate; acting additional under secretary to government of Madras in the revenue (famine) department, May, 1877, to April, 1879; transferred permanently to the Madras civil service, Sept., 1878; on special duty preparing Madras administration report, Sept., 1879; acting Canarese translator to government, Feb., 1880; acting assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, July, 1880; deputy superintendent of census operations, Sept., 1880; sub-collector and joint magistrate, South Arcot, May, 1886; collector and magistrate, Aug., 1889.

STOKES, HENRY EDWARD, C.S.I., B.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Minehead Diocesan School, Kilkenny College, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 4th Jan., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1866; under secretary

to government of Madras in the departments under the chief secretary, Aug., 1869; acting collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, Jan., 1871; fellow of the University of Madras, Feb., 1875; sub-secretary, board of revenue, July 1875; on special duty under government of India, with the Madras salt commission, Jan.-Sept., 1876; secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878, and additional secretary on famine duties, Feb., 1877; district and sessions judge, South Malabar, Oct., 1879; collector and magistrate, Salem, June, 1881; member of forest-draft bill committee, April, 1882; member of local self-government committee, June, 1882; secretary to government of Madras in the revenue department, Jan., 1884; acting chief secretary to government, Oct., 1886; additional member of council, Madras, for making laws and regulations, Oct., 1886; chief secretary to government, Oct., 1887; temporary member of governor's council, July, 1888; appointed ordinary member and assumed office, 9th Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1889.

STOKES, HENRY JOHN, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at St. Columba College, Rath-farnham, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; assistant collector and magistrate, Oct., 1863; served in Bombay from Oct., 1864, to Jan., 1870, and subsequently in Madras, and was appointed principal assistant, July, 1871, district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1877, and collector and magistrate, Madura, June, 1879; on special famine duty, Madura, Aug., 1877; district and sessions judge, Madura, Jan., 1884; collector and magistrate, North Arcot, Oct., 1884; member of draft tenancy bill committee, July, 1885; district and sessions judge, Coimbatore, May, 1886; on deputation as a member of the public service commission, Nov., 1886; on special duty in connection with rent recovery bill commission; retired, July, 1889.

STOKES, WHITLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., Hon. D.C.L., Oxon., LL.D., Dublin, Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, late of Legislative Department of the Government of India.—Reporter to the high court and acting administrator-general, Madras, 1863-4; served subsequently as assistant secretary to government of India, legislative branch of home department, secretary to the governor-general's legislative council, and secretary to the government of India in the legislative department; law member of the council of the governor-general, May, 1877, to May, 1882; president of Indian law commission, 1879; in 1868, framed the scheme for collecting, copying, and cataloguing the Sanskrit MSS. preserved in India, in recognition of which he was made honorary member of the Deutsche Morgenländische Gesellschaft; correspondent of the Institut de France (Académie des Inscriptions et Belles Lettres), fellow of Calcutta University, and honorary fellow of Jesus College, Oxford; author of 'A Treatise on the liens of legal Practitioners,' 'On Powers of Attorney' ('Bythewood and Jarman's Conveyancing,' vol. VIII., part 1); 'Hindu Law Books,' Madras, 1865; the 'Indian Succession Act, with a Commentary,' Calcutta, 1865; the 'Indian Companies Act, 1866, with Notes'; the 'Older Statutes in force in India, with Notes,' 1874; the 'Unrepealed General Acts of the

Governor-General of India, with Chronological Tables,' &c., 3 vols., Calcutta, 1875 and 1876, the 'Anglo-Indian Codes,' vol. I., 1887, and vol. II., 1888 (Clarendon Press, Oxford); the 'Tripartite life of Patrick, with other documents relating to that Saint,' 1887; and various philosophical works published in Germany, France, and England.

STONE, SAMUEL JAMES, Police Department, Punjab.—Appointed assistant district superintendent of police, 10th March, 1861; district superintendent, Nov., 1872; served in Assam from Dec., 1884, to March, 1885, when for a short time he was on special duty at Rawalpindi; resumed his post in the Punjab, April, 1885; promoted to 1st grade, Sept., 1888.

STOREY, HENRY FRANCIS, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, Nov., 1868, as executive engineer on the Punjab northern state railway; transferred to the Neemuch-Nasirabad railway surveys, 1878, and subsequently to the Indus Valley railway; engineer-in-chief, Kandahar state railway, March, 1880; engineer-in-chief, Indus Valley and Kandahar railways, Oct., 1881; superintendent of way and works, Sukkur, 1883; officiating manager, Indus Valley railway, Nov., 1885; superintending engineer, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; superintending engineer, 3rd class, May, 1886; 2nd class, Feb., 1888; officiating secretary to resident, Hyderabad, June, 1888; confirmed in that appointment, Dec., 1889; 1st superintending engineer, Dec., 1889.

STRACHAN, RIGHT REV. JOHN MILLER, D.D., M.D., Ecclesiastical Department, Burma.—Appointed Bishop of Rangoon, 30th June, 1882.

STRACHEY, SIR JOHN, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1842; served in the N.W. Provinces in the grades of assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector; senior assistant, Kumaon, 1848; magistrate and collector, Moradabad, 1854; officiating commissioner of Kumaon, 1861; president of commission to inquire into cholera epidemic of 1861; judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, 1862; president of the sanitary commission with government of India, 1864; chief commissioner, Oudh, 1866; member of governor-general's council, 7th March, 1868; acting viceroy and governor-general on death of Earl of Mayo, 1872; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, 1874; retired from the civil service, 1876; financial member of the governor-general's council, 23rd Dec., 1876, to 1880; left India, Dec., 1880; member of council of India, 14th July, 1885; joint author with Lieutenant-General R. Strachey of 'The Finances and Public Works of India,' 1882; author of 'India,' 1888.

STRACHEY, RICHARD, Lieutenant-General, R.E., C.S.I., F.R.S.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1836; was employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces from 1840, and appointed executive engineer, Ganges canal, July, 1843; under secretary to government of India, public works department, 1857; secretary to government, Central Provinces, 1857; consulting engineer, railway department, Sept., 1858; secretary to government of India, public works department, 1862; inspector-general of irrigation, and chief engineer, 1st class, Dec., 1866; additional member of governor-general's council, 1869; inspecting engineer of machinery

and stores for state railways, India office, 1871; member of council of India, 1875, to 12th July, 1878; special duty in India, 1878; officiating financial member of council of governor-general, 1879; officiating member of council of governor-general, 1880; president of the famine commission, 1880; reappointed member of council of India, 1879; president, royal geographical society, 1887; retired from the Council of India on his appointment as chairman of the East India railway company, 1889.

STRAFFORD (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. GEORGE HENRY CHARLES BYNG.—Born in 1830; educated at Eton and Oxford; sat in the House of Commons for Tavistock, 1852–1857, and for Midlesex, 1857, to Jan., 1874, when he succeeded to the upper house, as Viscount Enfield; attached to Earl Russell's special mission to Vienna, 1855; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, 1865, till July, 1866; under secretary for foreign affairs, Dec., 1870; under secretary of state for India, Sept., 1880, to Dec., 1882; succeeded to the earldom of Strafford in 1886.

STRAHAN, CHARLES, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Survey of India Department.—First commission, Bengal engineers, June, 1860; arrived in India, Oct., 1862; joined the survey department as military 2nd assistant, Sept., 1863; served with the Bhutan field force in charge of reconnoitring party, Oct., 1865, to July, 1866 (medal and clasp); surveyor, 3rd grade, Dec., 1867; served with the Peshawur field force in Afghanistan, 1879 (medal, brevet of major); deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, Jan., 1884; assistant surveyor-general in charge of cartographic and engraving branches, May, 1884; deputy superintendent, 1st grade, April, 1889, officiating also as deputy surveyor-general.

STRAHAN, GEORGE, Colonel, R.E., Survey of India Department.—Obtained commission from Addiscombe, Dec., 1858; arrived in India, Nov., 1860; served under the military department, and also in the irrigation branch, public works department, till July, 1862, when he was appointed to the survey department; served as assistant in the Gwalior and Central India survey, and afterwards held charges in the Rajputana, Mysore, and Khandesh surveys; deputy superintendent, Nov., 1867; officiating assistant surveyor-general, in charge of engraving and cartographic branches, Calcutta, April–July, 1883; deputy superintendent, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; on duty in Europe, April–Aug., 1885; deputy surveyor-general (trigonometrical branch), Oct., 1889.

STRAIGHT, DOUGLAS, B.L., Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple, Nov., 1865; counsel to London banks association from 1868; junior counsel, treasury prosecution, from 1877; sat in House of Commons as member for Shrewsbury, 1870 to 1874; appointed one of the royal commissioners to inquire into corrupt election practices at Boston, 1875; puisne judge, high court, N.W. Provinces, June, 1879; fellow of Calcutta University, June, 1883, and of the Allahabad University, Nov., 1887; officiated as chief justice of the N.W. Provinces high court in 1883, 1884, and 1886.

STRATTON, JOHN PROUDFOOT, Brigade Surgeon, M.D., late Bombay Medical Establishment.—Assistant surgeon, Bombay medical service,

Oct., 1852; between 1854 and 1859 was employed as residency surgeon, Baroda, also serving as assistant resident, 1857–59; from Sept., 1859, served as assistant resident in Bundelcund; political agent, Bundelcund, June, 1864; surgeon-major, Oct., 1872; political agent, Meywar, 1881; resident, Western states, Rajputana, July, 1881, and in the Eastern Rajputana states, 1882, till Dec., 1885, when his services were replaced at disposal of military department; retired, Dec., 1886.

STREATFIELD, HENRY CUTHBERT, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Marlborough College and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under-secretary to the government of Bengal, financial and municipal department, June, 1890; confirmed, Nov. 1890.

STREET, B. H. G., Store Department, India Office.—Writer, 30th June, 1874; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depot, Oct., 1883.

STREET, HENRY ALFRED, Captain (Indian Marine), Presidency Port Officer, Madras.—Commander of 'Tenasserim,' Dec., 1876–June, 1880; assistant secretary to government of India, military (marine) department, June, 1880 to March, 1882; on duty in Bombay, 1882–84; assistant director Indian marine, April, 1884; 1st grade commander, Dec., 1886; port officer, Madras, Dec., 1887.

STRICKLAND, WILLIAM ALEXANDER WICDE, Lieutenant, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—1st commission, Oct., 1880; assistant commissioner, Aug., 1886.

STROVER, GEORGE AUGUSTUS, Colonel, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Joined the service as ensign, Jan., 1857; arrived at Madras, Feb., 1857; served in the Burma commission from 14th April, 1862, as assistant commissioner and cantonment magistrate; officiating magistrate and small cause court judge of Rangoon, 1866; superintendent of hill tribes, North Arakan, Sept., 1868; from Aug., 1869, to Sept., 1875, served in the political department as assistant political agent at Bhamo, and political agent at Mandalay (Jan., 1872); from Nov., 1877, served as deputy commissioner of Toungheo, and acted as commissioner of Arakan, Aug. to Nov., 1883; 1st grade deputy commissioner, July, 1881; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1885; commissioner, May, 1889.

STUART, ANDREW EDMUND CASTLE-STUART, F.G.S., F.L.S., F.R.G.S., Madras C.S.—Educated at Wellington College and Kingstown School, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; special assistant to the collectors of Nilgiris and Malabar for settlement purposes, Aug., 1886; head assistant to collector and magistrate, July, 1888; special settlement officer, Malabar and S. Canara, June, 1891.

STUART, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Postal Department, Bombay.—Served from 23rd Nov., 1855, as postmaster in Mysore, Madras, and Hyderabad; presidency postmaster, Madras, Jan., 1864; from March, 1865, served in Bombay as presidency postmaster, and also officiated for

various periods as postmaster-general, Bombay, Punjab, and Madras.

STUART, CHARLES JOSEPH, Colonel, I.S.C., late Madras Revenue Department.—Arrived in India, 21st Aug., 1852; served in the Madras revenue settlement department from April, 1861, as assistant director and deputy director; special assistant to collector of Kurnool for survey and settlement purposes, April, 1871, to Nov., 1873, and again in Chingleput, Dec., 1873, and in Madura, April, 1882; 1st grade of deputy directors, June, 1883; retired, 1889.

STUART, GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Madras Educational Department.—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Aug., 1882, as principal, Kumbakonum College; has acted at various periods as principal, Presidency College, and as professor of English literature and of mathematics there.

STUART, HAROLD ARTHUR, Madras C.S.—Educated at High School, Bishop's Stortford, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1881, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant secretary to government in the judicial and legislative departments (Dec., 1884); acting under secretary to government, revenue department, Jan., 1886; sub. pro tem. under secretary in the chief secretary's department, Oct., 1886; under secretary to government, revenue department, Sept., 1888, and chief-secretary's department, Nov., 1888; acting private secretary to Governor of Madras, Nov.—Dec., 1889; provincial superintendent of census operations, March, 1890.

STUART, SIR ROBERT, Knt., Q.C., late High Court, N.W. Provinces.—Educated at Perth, and afterwards at the University of Edinburgh; called to the Scotch bar, July, 1840; and to the English bar at Lincoln's Inn, June, 1856; practised at the chancery bar as an equity draughtsman and conveyancer, and as an advising counsel; practised also in the House of Lords and privy council, and before parliamentary committees of both houses until 1868, when he was appointed Q.C., and soon after elected a bencher of his inn; appointed chief justice of the North-West Provinces, India, and knighted, August, 1871; retired, March, 1884; is a member of the general council of the University of Edinburgh, and has been a frequent contributor to periodical literature, especially on legal subjects; author of many legal pamphlets.

STUART, R. G., late Political Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 19th Feb., 1873; clerk specially attached to the political and secret department, Feb., 1876; retired, April, 1891.

STULPNAGEL, CHARLES REBSCH, Ph.D., M.A., Education Department, Punjab.—Served in the Punjab education department from 25th April, 1867, in the Zillah schools at Peshawur, Delhi and Lahore, and in the government college, Lahore; assistant professor, government college, Lahore, Jan., 1875; was appointed professor, July, 1875, and officiated for various periods as principal of the college; inspector of schools, Lahore circle, May, 1885; 2nd grade of the education department, Feb.,

1886; inspector of schools, May, 1886; principal, Government College, Lahore, Nov., 1891.

STURROCK, JOHN, Madras C.S.—Educated at the High School, Dundee; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1866; acting secretary to government in the revenue department, Nov., 1873; boundary commissioner between Canara and Mysore, May, 1873, and again in 1880-81; Canarese translator to government, Jan., 1874; under secretary to government in the chief secretary's department, July, 1875; additional sub-secretary, board of revenue, for special duty with famine commission, Nov., 1878, also acting as secretary, Jan., 1879; sub-secretary, board of revenue, Oct., 1879; collector and magistrate, Oct., 1883; acting 4th member of board of revenue and commissioner of salt revenue, &c., July, 1888.

STURT, W. NEVILLE, Political Department, India Office.—Educated at Repton; temporary clerk, 1st May, 1862; junior clerk, store department, 1862; accountant-general's department, 1863; military department, 1863; private secretary to parliamentary under-secretary of state, 1863-64, and 1868-77; senior clerk, revenue department, April, 1877; transferred temporarily to political and secret department, April, 1890.

SULLIVAN, HENRY EDWARD, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1850; served in revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed small cause court judge, Cuddalore, Sept., 1866; civil and sessions judge, Cuddalah, Dec., 1866; collector and magistrate, Bellary, March, 1868, and subsequently of Trichinopoly; officiating director of revenue settlement, Feb., 1874; acting member, board of revenue, and president of the committee for examination of assistants, April, 1876; in charge of the Inam department, May, 1876; acting resident of Travancore and Cochin, March, 1877, to Feb., 1878; board of revenue, April, 1878; famine commission, Jan., 1879; 1st member, board of revenue, and commissioner, uncovenanted civil service examinations, Jan., 1879; on duty studying records of famine commission, April, 1879; on special duty in Godaveri district, investigating causes of the disturbances in Rumpar country, July, 1879; on duty to England as member of famine commission, Oct., 1879, to July, 1880; additional member of council for making laws and regulations, Jan., 1881; in charge of settlement department, Feb., 1881; ordinary member of council, June, 1882; president of local self-government committee, June, 1882; retired, Dec., 1886.

SURYA KUMAR AGASTI, M.A., Bengal C.S.—Appointed under the native civil service rules assistant magistrate and collector, Purneah, on probation, March, 1885; confirmed in the statutory civil service, April, 1887.

SUTTON, E. W., Political Department, India Office.—Second division clerk, Census office, 7th April, 1891; 2nd class clerk, India office, 1st September, 1891.

SWAIN, W. H., B.Sc., Military Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th June, 1881; B.Sc. (Honours), London University, 1888.

SWETENHAM, EDMUND, Colonel, I.S.C., late Public Works Department, North-Western

Provinces.—Joined public works department from Roorkee college, Nov., 1853, and served in the Punjab; resigned on obtaining commission in the army, March, 1857; served in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1858 (medal); reappointed to public works department, Feb., 1859, and served in N.W. Provinces, buildings and roads branch; executive engineer, 1st grade, Sept., 1869; transferred to British Burma, May, 1872; transferred to Central India, and served from Sept., 1874, to Aug., 1880, as executive engineer, Mhow division, and acted for various periods as chief engineer, and secretary to governor-general's agent; transferred to N.W. Provinces, buildings and roads branch, Aug., 1880, and promoted to superintending engineer, 3rd class, Sept., 1880; and class, July, 1884; 1st class, permanent, April, 1888; retired Feb., 1890.

SYAD ALAY MAHOMED, Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Acting extra assistant commissioner, 4th class, 26th Jan., 1881; probationer for the native civil service, Dec., 1882; appointed member of the civil service, and supernumerary assistant commissioner, Dec., 1883.

SYAD NUR-UD-DIN KHAN, Bombay C.S.—Served in Hyderabad from May, 1879, as attaché to Resident, judge of small causes, &c.; assistant commissioner, Jan., 1883; under secretary to Hyderabad state, Jan., 1885; appointed a probationer under native civil service rules, March, 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1888.

SYKES, ROBERT, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Giggleswick Grammar School and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1888; and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; on special duty, Nov., 1889; held charge of Gujranwala jail, Jan., 1890.

SYKES-GAMBLE, JAMES, M.A., F.L.S., Forest Department, North-Western Provinces.—Educated at Magdalen college, Oxford; appointed assistant conservator of forests, Oct., 1871; arrived in India 27th Dec., 1871, and served in British Burma; transferred to Bengal, Aug., 1872; served under the government of India as assistant to the inspector-general of forests, April, 1877, to Jan., 1879; deputy conservator, Bengal, July, 1880; conservator, 3rd grade, Sept., 1882; transferred to Madras, Dec., 1882; conservator, 2nd grade, Dec., 1884; transferred to N.W. Provinces as conservator of school circle, and director of the Forest school, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1890.

SYMES, EDWARD SPENCE, C.I.E., Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1875, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma, Aug., 1876; served as assistant commissioner, and was placed on special duty in the secretariat, July and Nov., 1878; on special forest demarcation duty, Feb., 1879; personal assistant to chief commissioner, May, 1879; junior secretary to chief commissioner, June, 1881; also officiated as secretary to the chief commissioner for various periods, and as settlement secretary, March-May, 1882; secre-

tary to chief commissioner and officer in charge of Delhi state prisoners, Apr. 1886; C.I.E., May, 1886; chief secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1887; commissioner, Minbu, July, 1890; resumed post as chief secretary, Dec., 1890.

SYMNS, ROBERT CORSER MONTFORT, Bengal C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Westminster, King's College School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service (Burma) after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

SYMONDS, WILLIAM POWELL, Bombay C.S.—Educated at Hereford Cathedral School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; member of the central committee for departmental examinations, April, 1884; acting under secretary to government, revenue, financial, and general departments, 1884; forest settlement officer, March, 1887; 2nd assistant collector, and acting director of land records and agriculture, Aug., 1887; acting first assistant collector and magistrate, Poona, Nov., 1887; on special duty, Kathiawar, March, 1889; postmaster-general of Bombay, May, 1889.

SZCZEPANSKI, HENRY CHARLES ANTHONY, Colonel, I.S.C., Berar Commission.—First commission, Bengal infantry, June, 1857; arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1857; was employed under the military department up to 1860, including service during the mutiny (medal); assistant commissioner, Punjab, July, 1860; on special famine duty, Nov., 1861; transferred to Hyderabad assigned districts, Nov., 1864, and served as assistant commissioner, cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, and deputy commissioner (1st class, Feb., 1885); officiated as judicial commissioner at various periods, and as commissioner in 1888 and 1889.

TABOR, FRANCIS SAMUEL, Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at St. Paul's School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

TAHEIRUDDIN AHMED, B.L., Bengal C.S.—Appointed probationer under the native civil service rules, 6th March, 1890.

TALBOT, ADELBERT CECIL, Major, C.I.E., I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, royal artillery, Jan., 1867; joined the Bengal staff corps, 1869, and served with 4th cavalry, Hyderabad contingent; on special duty with the Afghan commissioner, Seistan Arbitration, 1872-73; officiating assistant agent, Rajputana, 1873; attaché, secretariat, foreign department, 1874; officiating assistant secretary to the government of India, legislative department, and officiating secretary to board of examiners, Calcutta, 1875-76; acting assistant resident, and assistant-general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, Hyderabad, 1878; 1st assistant agent, Rajputana, Feb., 1878; fellow of Calcutta University, March, 1879; additional political agent, Eastern Rajputana states, 1881; political agent, Bhurtpore and Kerowlee, 1882; political agent, Bikanir, 1883; graded as additional political agent, 1st class; officiated as resident in Turkish Arabia, June, 1883; political agent in Bikanir, May, 1889; officiating resident,

Persian Gulf, March, 1891; edited a revised edition of 'Aitchison's Treaties,' 1876.

TALBOT, GERALD H., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at St. Peter's College, Radley; supervisor, 1st grade, India store depot, 19th June, 1877.

TALBOT, WALTER STANLEY, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Wellington College and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1838; arrived 31st Oct., 1839, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

TANNER, HENRY CHARLES BASKERVILLE, Colonel, Indian S.C., late Survey of India.—First commission, Bombay artillery, June, 1854; accompanied the Persian expeditionary force of 1855 (medal and clasp); joined the survey department as assistant in the Sind revenue survey, Oct., 1862; was afterwards employed in the Central Provinces revenue survey, and was in charge of the Bombay city survey, Dec., 1869; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, April, 1870; was subsequently employed in the Bhawalpur revenue survey, and the Deccan survey, and accompanied the Lushai expedition in 1871-72, and the Khyber column in the Afghan campaign, 1878-79; was afterwards in charge of the Darjeeling and Nepal boundary parties, Nov., 1882; deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, Oct., 1884; employed with the Tibet mission from May, 1885; reverted to military duty, 1890.

TATE, ALEXANDER CHAMBERS, M.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; head assistant, Sept., 1888; senior assistant, Nov., 1889; head assistant, Tanjore, Jan., 1891.

TATE, WILLIAM JACOB, Madras C.S.—Educated at Repton; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1874, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; head assistant collector, Salem, Jan., 1883; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1889; district and sessions judge, March, 1890.

TAUNTON, A. G., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at the Jesuits' College, Tourney; writer, India store depot, 29th July, 1868; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

TAWNEY, CHARLES HENRY, C.I.E., M.A., Educational Service, Bengal.—Joined the department, Nov., 1864, and served as professor, Presidency College, up to Nov., 1876, when he was appointed principal of the college, and promoted to 1st class of the educational service; officiating director of public instruction, Aug., 1879, and again in 1885, 1887, and 1889; is also registrar of the Calcutta University.

TAWNEY, JAMES WALTER, late Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Repton School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1863; served as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner, Bhoonan, till Oct., 1865, and subsequently in the Cen-

tral Provinces as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; also acted as small cause court judge, and cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, in 1873, and as small cause court judge of Nagpur in 1876; deputy commissioner, 4th class, Oct., 1881; retired, April, 1889.

TAYLER, SKIPWITH HENRY CHURCHILL, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 2nd March, 1853; between July, 1854, and Sept., 1872, served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector, 1st grade; from Sept., 1872, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, 1st grade, March, 1875; retired, April, 1887.

TAYLER, WILLIAM VANSITTART GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 6th Nov., 1855; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Pubna, Oct., 1866; retired, 1890.

TAYLOR, SIR ALEXANDER, G.C.B., General, R.E.—Educated at Hofuzl, Switzerland, and Addiscombe military college; 1st commission, 9th June, 1843; major, Jan., 1858; colonel, March, 1864; lieutenant-general, Oct., 1877; and general (honorary rank), Dec., 1878; entered the Indian public works department, 26th Dec., 1846, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Jan., 1848; superintendent of the Lahore and Peshawur road, Dec., 1849; superintending engineer, Nov., 1856; officiating secretary to the government of the Punjab, public works department, Jan., 1865, to Jan., 1867, and Feb., 1871, to Feb., 1872; chief engineer, April, 1872; deputy inspector-general of military works, 1870-1878; president of the defence committee of India, 1876, 1877, 1878; president of the royal Indian engineering college, Cooper's Hill, from Nov., 1880; served throughout the Sutlej campaign (medal with clasps for Ferozeshah and Sobraon), and throughout the Punjab campaign, 1848-9, being present at the action of Sooraj Khoond, the capture of Mooltan, and the battle of Goojerat (medal with 2 clasps); served throughout the Indian mutiny of 1857-8; present at the operations before, and at the siege and capture of Delhi; commanded the Bengal engineers at siege and capture of Lucknow (medal with 2 clasps); served during the Umbeyla campaign, 1863; C.B. in 1858, K.C.B. in 1877, and G.C.B. in 1889.

TAYLOR, FRANKLIN EDEN, Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1886; and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner.

TAYLOR, FREDERICK BEATSON, B.A., Bengal C.S.—Educated at Christ's Hospital, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1874; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, Apr., 1886; 1st grade, Jan., 1888; has also officiated as district and sessions judge.

TAYLOR, GEORGE NOBLE, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from

Haileybury College, in 1840; served in the grades of assistant and head assistant collector and magistrate, and was appointed secretary to the college and university boards, and assistant collector of customs at Madras in special charge of the salt department, 1849; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Rajamundry, 1851; acted as collector and magistrate of Nellore in 1857, and of South Arcot in 1858; Inam commissioner, 1858; special commissioner for railways, Calcutta, to report upon the affairs of the East Indian railway, May, 1863, and subsequently held a similar post at Bombay; provisional member of governor-general's council, 1864; ordinary member of council, March, 1865; retired, March, 1870.

TAYLOR, HERBERT DOUGLAS, Madras C.S.—Educated at Winchester, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1833; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1835; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1836; manager of Jeypore Estate, Feb., 1890.

TAYLOR, JAMES, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the financial department, 1st Sept., 1865, and served in Bengal as assistant to the accountant-general, and deputy accountant-general; assistant comptroller-general, June, 1879.

TAYLOR, S. A., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Epsom College; junior clerk, India office, 31st Jan., 1867; senior clerk, Jan., 1890.

TEAGUE, GEORGE F., Funds Department, India Office.—Senior book-keeper, funds department, India office, 28th Sept., 1866; head book-keeper, March, 1873.

TEMPLE, HENRY MARTINDALE, Captain, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Sub-lieutenant, 41st regiment, June, 1872; arrived in India, Sept., 1872; extra aide-de-camp to lieutenant-governor, Bengal, Dec., 1875; junior attaché, foreign department, government of India, March, 1876; senior attaché, June, 1877; cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, Feb., 1878; assistant to resident, Hyderabad, and assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1880; officiating judicial superintendent of railways, Nizam's Dominions, June, 1881; assistant to governor-general's agent, Baroda, Nov., 1882; boundary settlement officer, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1884; political agent, Kalat, May, 1885; political assistant, 1st class, March, 1886; political agent, South Eastern Baluchistan, May, 1887; and at Kalat, Nov., 1887.

TEMPLE, JAMES HERBERT, Bengal C.S.—Educated at Burnley Grammar School, Repton School, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed member of the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India 20th Dec., 1889; and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

TEMPLE, JOHN ALEXANDER, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, Dec., 1860; arrived in India, 8th Aug., 1861; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and acted at various times as cantonment magistrate, Saugor, Jubbulpore, and Kamptee; small cause court judge, Jubbulpore, May, 1872-74, and again, 1877; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Nov., 1884; 2nd class, sub-

pro tem., Feb., 1888, and permanent, Sept., 1839.

TEMPLE, SIR RICHARD, Bart., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., D.C.L. (Oxon), LL.D. (Cantab), late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1846; served in the N.W. Provinces and Punjab in the grades of assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector; settlement officer, trans-Sutlej division, 1852; secretary to chief commissioner, Punjab, 1854; commissioner, Lahore, 1859; chief commissioner of currency and chief assistant to financial member of council, 1860; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, 1865; resident, Hyderabad, 1867; member of governor-general's council, 25th April, 1868; president of the statistical committee, 1868; lieutenant governor of Bengal, 1874; governor of Bombay, 30th April, 1877, to 1880; retired from the civil service in 1877; chairman of the board of visitors, Royal Indian Engineering College, Cooper's Hill, 1880; in the general election of Nov., 1885, was returned for Evesham, and again in the election of July, 1886; has been a member of the London School Board since 1884; vice-chairman, 1885-8; author of 'Men and Events of my Time in India,' 1882, 'Oriental Experience,' 1883, &c.

TEMPLE, RICHARD CARNAC, Major, I.S.C.—First commission, 4th Jan., 1871; from 11th Aug., 1879, served in the Punjab as assistant cantonment magistrate, and cantonment magistrate, and was promoted to 1st class magistrate, Oct., 1882; transferred to Burma as cantonment magistrate, Mandalay, March, 1887; assistant commissioner, April, 1887; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1888; provisional superintendent of census, 1890; president of the Rangoon Municipality, Feb., 1891; author of 'Notes on the Transliteration of the Burmese Alphabet,' 'A dissertation on the proper names of the Punjabis,' 'Wide awake stories' (Punjabi and Cashmiri Folk Tales), and other works; also founded and conducted 'Indian Notes and Queries,' and edited the 'Indian Antiquary.'

TENNANT, JAMES FRANCIS, Lieutenant-General, C.I.E., R.E.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1847; arrived in India, 7th March, 1849; assistant engineer, Ganges canal, 1849 to 1851; assistant in the great trigonometrical survey of India, 1851 to 1859; was garrison engineer to the Delhi field force, and served at the siege and assault of Delhi, and at the siege and capture of Lucknow (medal with two clasps, and brevet of major); government astronomer at Madras, Oct. 1859, to Oct., 1860; commandant, sappers and miners, Oct., 1860, to Sept., 1863; served in the public works department, Sept., 1863, to July, 1871, as executive engineer in Burma, and in the Punjab and Bengal; while on leave was appointed to observe eclipse of the sun, 1867-68, and was deputed for a similar duty in 1871; served as mint master, Calcutta, from July, 1871, to Jan., 1884; on special duty in England, Feb.-April, 1872; superintended the observations of the transit of Venus at Roorkee and Lahore, 1876; C.I.E., Jan., 1879; retired from military service and civil duties on 10th Jan., 1884; is a fellow of the Royal Society, and of the Royal Astronomical Society.

TEUNON, WILLIAM, Bengal C.S. (Assam).—Educated at University of Aberdeen, and King's

College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 24th Dec., 1883; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1884; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, Jan., 1888.

THOM, WILLIAM, Surgeon-General, F.R.C.S., late Bombay Medical Service.—Educated at Marischal College, and University of Aberdeen, and at University College, London, and in Paris; F.R.C.S., England; fellow of university of Bombay; assistant surgeon on the Bombay establishment, 1841; served under the military department, first with the artillery, and then with the 3rd regiment native infantry, the 2nd Belooch battalion, the Bombay fusiliers, and the 17th native infantry at Baroda; surgeon, 1854; served subsequently at Belgaum and Poona; in medical charge, marine battalion, Bombay, May, 1860; garrison surgeon, and surgeon to the European general hospital, 1861; served on the medical board of the India office, 1866-67; deputy inspector-general of hospitals, March, 1867; surgeon-general, Feb., 1874; retired, Sept., 1877.

THOMAS, GEORGE EBENEZER, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Burma.—Appointed from the East India railway as assistant engineer, 28th Aug., 1867, and posted to the Central Provinces; transferred to the Punjab northern state railway, March, 1868; locomotive superintendent, Punjab northern state railway, April, 1875; executive engineer, April, 1876; transferred to Indus Valley state railway, Nov., 1878; 2nd class, revenue establishment, July, 1879; transferred to British Burma, Nov., 1881; class 1, grade 3, revenue scale, Jan., 1887; retired 1880.

THOMAS, HENRY SULLIVAN, F.L.S., F.Z.S., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector, and joint magistrate, acting collector of sea customs, member of the board of examiners, and acting civil and sessions judge; from May, 1867, to Sept., 1874, served as collector and magistrate of South Canara, and subsequently at Tanjore, and as political agent for French settlement at Karikal; from April, 1878, served on the board of revenue, Madras, and acted as secretary to government in the revenue department in 1878-79; in charge of the Inam department, Jan.-April, 1879; president, board of examiners, Oct., 1881; on special duty as a member of the local self-government committee, Sept., 1882; on duty to Calcutta as an additional member of the governor-general's council for laws and regulations, Dec., 1882, and again, Dec., 1883; on special duty in connection with the pearl fisheries at Tuticorin, and deputed to study system of management in force in Ceylon, March-May, 1884; 1st member, board of revenue, July, 1884; on deputation to Tanjore district to report on necessity for granting remissions, April, 1885; fellow of Madras University, March, 1886; on special duty to Madura, March to Sept., 1886, and to Delhi, to attend conference on agricultural and veterinary questions, March, 1888; on special duty drawing Fisheries Bill, April, 1888; retired, Jan., 1889; has published 'A Report on Pisciculture in South Canara,' 1870; 'The Rod in India,' 1881; and 'A Report on Pearl Fisheries and Chank Fisheries,' 1884.

THOMAS, ROBERT MOSELY BRYCE, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission as ensign, 6th Jan., 1859; arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1859; from Oct., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting deputy commissioner, and acting small cause court judge, and was appointed cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, Nov., 1876, and subsequently at Kamptee; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Dec., 1885.

THOMAS, WILLIAM CHARSLEY, Lieut.-Colonel, Police Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service as officiating adjutant of military police, Mainpuri, Dec., 1858, having previously served as a volunteer in General Havelock's cavalry, 1857-58; adjutant of military police, Oct., 1859; inspector of district police, Jan., 1861; district superintendent of police, May, 1861; officiated as assistant inspector-general of the government railway police, July-Oct., 1868; on special duty with the camp of the lieutenant-governor, Dec., 1876, and again in March, 1878, with the governor-general's camp; 1st grade superintendent, Lucknow, April, 1885; junior deputy inspector-general of police, May, 1886; senior, Jan., 1890.

THOMASON, CHARLES SIMEON, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1852; joined the public works department, July, 1855, as deputy superintendent, Ganges canal; during the mutiny served at the battle of Budlekeserai and the siege of Delhi (medal and clasp); executive engineer, Oct., 1858; superintendent Rohilkund canals, Dec., 1864; officiating superintendent engineer and deputy secretary, public works department, N.W. Provinces, Sept., 1873; on special duty to Madras, in connection with harbour work, 1877-79; superintending engineer and secretary to governor-general's agent, Central India, Aug., 1880; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1882; retired, May, 1888.

THOMPSON, ARTHUR, M.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at St. Cuthbert's, Ushaw, and University of London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1876, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting principal assistant, assistant sessions judge, acting sub-collector, and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate, and district and sessions judge, head assistant collector, Cuddapah, Nov., 1884; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, N. Malabar, June, 1891.

THOMPSON, C. T., Registry Department, India Office.—Educated at the City of London School; clerk, 2nd class, India office, 19th March, 1883.

THOMPSON, DANIEL ROBERT, Surgeon-Major, M.D., C.I.E., M.R.C.S., and L.M., late Madras Medical Service.—Acting civil surgeon, Tellicherry, April, 1865, to Sept., 1866; joined the Indian medical service as assistant surgeon, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1867; and served under the military department, also acting as Zillah surgeon and superintendent of jail at Chingleput, April, 1868; and at Tinnevelly, April, 1869, to Sept., 1870; in medical charge of garrison, lock hospital, and meteorological observatories, Bellary, Dec., 1873; resident

surgeon, general hospital, Madras, and professor of pathology and materia medica in Medical College, March, 1875; from May, 1875, served as surgeon, 1st district, Madras, and medical inspector of emigrants; also superintendent of auxiliary provincial medical school, and native infirmary, Royapooram; C.I.E. for special famine services in 1876-78, 1st Jan., 1879; surgeon major, April, 1879; retired, May, 1888.

THOMPSON, DAVID WILLIAM, Educational Department, Punjab.—Served in the education department from March, 1862, as master in the Zillah Schools at Lahore and Amritsar; inspector of schools, May, 1870; 3rd grade of the department, Nov., 1883.

THOMPSON, FREDERICK C., Public Works Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, accounts branch, 9th March, 1872; public works department, Aug., 1875; editor of India Office List, 1885-7; senior clerk, 1887.

THOMPSON, GEORGE, B.A., Educational Department, Central Provinces.—Head master, Saugor high school, Sept., 1869, to Oct., 1875; served from Oct., 1875, as inspector of schools, Central Provinces; was deputed to attend educational conferences at Naini Tal in 1885 and 1886; officiating inspector-general of education, May to Nov., 1889.

THOMPSON, HENRY EDWARD, M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as Morse assistant, 12th May, 1857; arrived in India, 12th Sept., 1857; superintendent, Dec., 1866; has held charge of various divisions, including Malabar, Bangalore, Arakan, Nagpur, and Rajputana.

THOMPSON, ROSS, Colonel R.E., Public Works Department, Madras.—First commission, June, 1858; arrived in India, 14th Jan., 1861; joined the public works department, Aug., 1863; executive engineer, April, 1867; district engineer, presidency, April, 1875; consulting architect to government, May, 1875; commanded Queen's Own sappers and miners throughout the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); rejoined the public works department, Nov., 1880; acted as deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government, Oct., 1881; posted to West Coast division, Oct., 1884; presidency division, April, 1886; Nilgiri division, July, 1890; Trichinopoly division, Nov., 1890.

THOMSON, ALEXANDER, Education Department, N.W. Provinces.—Served in Oudh, Aug., 1866, to April, 1877, as inspector of schools, and acted as director of public instruction in 1866 and 1868; from April, 1877, served as professor of English literature in the Agra College, and acted for various periods as principal; confirmed, July, 1883.

THOMSON, ANDREW, Bengal, C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, Dec., 1885; served in Burma as assistant commissioner; transferred to Upper Burma on special duty, March, 1886; personal assistant to chief commissioner, March, 1887; officiated as under secretary to chief commissioner, Feb. to April, 1888.

THOMSON, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.B., Medical Department, Punjab.—Arrived in India, 7th April, 1866; served in military department to July, 1879;

services placed at the disposal of the Punjab government, and appointed officiating civil surgeon, 2nd class, Aug., 1879; confirmed in his appointment, Sept., 1880; medical adviser to H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala, in 1882, 1888, and 1889, and from Sept., 1890.

THOMSON, JAMES, M.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Grammar School, and University of Aberdeen; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; sub-collector, joint magistrate and principal assistant to collector, magistrate and agent, Godaveri, June, 1885; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1889.

THOMSON, JOHN BROWN, M.A., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Academy, and University, Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating magistrate and collector, officiating small cause court judge, and officiating cantonment magistrate; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Feb., 1882; deputy superintendent of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Nov., 1882; registrar of the high court, N.W. Provinces, May, 1884; magistrate and collector, April, 1889; officiated as secretary to government in 1889 and 1890.

THOMSON, ROBERT GEORGE, Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Islington Proprietary School, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; junior secretary to financial commissioner, Dec., 1882; officiating under secretary (revenue department) to government, Punjab, June, 1883; officiating junior secretary to government, Jan., 1884, and again in 1885 and 1886; senior secretary to financial commissioner, March, 1886; on special duty revising rules under the Punjab land revenue bill; postmaster-general, Punjab, Feb., 1888; junior secretary to Government of Punjab, and officiating revenue secretary, April, 1890.

THOMSON, SAMUEL JOHN, Surgeon-Major, Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service as surgeon, 1st Oct., 1877; arrived in India, 27th April, 1878; served on military, general, and jail duty, and as officiating deputy sanitary commissioner, and was confirmed in that office, May, 1885; officiated as sanitary commissioner in 1887 and 1888; author of 'Sanitary Principles.'

THORBURN, SEPTIMUS SMET, F.R.G.S., Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, officiating divisional judge, and settlement officer, and officiating commissioner and superintendent; judicial assistant, 1st grade, Jan., 1881; deputy commissioner, April, 1883; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Rawal Pindi, from April, 1886; author of 'Bannu, or Our Afghan Frontier,' 'David

Leslie,' and a report on the settlement of the Bannu district.

THORBURN, WALTER MILLER, B.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Dalhousie College, and University, Halifax, University of Edinburgh, and King's College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and acting district and sessions judge; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890.

THORNHILL, EDMUND BENSLEY, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; arrived in India, 16th Aug., 1854; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating small cause court judge; from Feb. to Sept., 1865, served in Oudh as officiating deputy commissioner, Fyzabad; magistrate and collector, Banda, March, 1872; civil and sessions judge, Banda, Nov., 1877; retired in 1885.

THORNHILL, GEORGE, C.S.I., late Madras Civil Service.—Educated at Rugby, and Haileybury, and appointed to the Madras civil service in 1842; arrived in Madras, 7th April, 1843; served as assistant to collector and magistrate in various districts, and was employed as civil officer in connection with the construction of the Godaveri and Kistna anicuts; principal assistant to collector and magistrate, Ganjam, 1856; collector and magistrate, Kistna, 1861; member of board of revenue, 1870-78, during which period he was frequently employed by government on special duties of various kinds; was also appointed director of bank of Madras, and commissioner for uncovenanted civil service examinations, 1875; retired, 1878.

THORNHILL, HENRY BEAUFROY, Captain, North-Western Provinces Administration.—1st commission, 23rd April, 1873; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1873; on field service, Afghanistan, Oct., 1878, to April, 1879 (medal); cantonment magistrate, Feb., 1887; appointed deputy superintendent, Port Blair, May, 1888; cantonment magistrate, N.W. Provinces, April, 1891.

THORNTON, ARTHUR PARRY, Major, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 14th foot, 14th Sept., 1867; arrived in India, Jan., 1868; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Sept., 1871; served under government of Bengal in Purneah and Burdwan as relief circle officer during famine, March-Dec., 1874; served under the foreign department of the government of India from Jan., 1875, and acted as assistant agent to governor-general, Rajputana, and as magistrate, Mount Abu, and assistant general superintendent for suppression of thuggee and dacoity; in charge of office of political agent, Mârwar, Jan.-March, 1877; attaché to foreign office during Delhi durbar, 1876-77; on military duty, 1878-79; from March, 1879, served under foreign department as cantonment magistrate at Morar, and at Nasirabad; assistant to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, Jan., 1882; cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, Secunderabad, Feb., 1884;

political agent, Bhopawur, May, 1886; Harowtee and Tonk, March, 1889.

THORNTON, EDWARD, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at the Charterhouse, and at Haileybury College, where he obtained honours, and from which he was appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1829; arrived in India, Feb., 1830; served in the N.W. Provinces, first as settlement officer, and afterwards as magistrate and collector; commissioner in the Punjab, Jhelum division, 1849-58; made C.B. for his services during the mutiny; judicial commissioner of the Punjab, 1858; retired, 1860.

THORNTON, JAMES HOWARD, Surgeon-Colonel, C.B., B.A. and M.D. (London), Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1856; from 9th Nov., 1861, served as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon, besides service under the military department; rank of surgeon-major, Jan., 1868, and brigade surgeon, March, 1882; principal medical officer with the Indian forces, Egypt, 1885; C.B., Aug., 1885; deputy surgeon-general, Punjab frontier force, June, 1886.

THORNTON, LESLIE MELVILLE, M.A., Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 1st Jan., 1881, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant secretary to government N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Oct., 1883; under secretary to government, Oct., 1886; officiated as financial secretary, Feb., 1891, and district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

THORNTON, THOMAS HENRY, C.S.I., D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's College, Oxford (1st class, moderations; Pusey and Ellerton Hebrew scholar; and 2nd class, final schools); appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition, 1855; assistant commissioner, Punjab, 1857-59; personal assistant to financial commissioner, Punjab, 1859-60; personal assistant to judicial commissioner, 1860-62; small cause court judge, Lahore, 1862-63; deputy commissioner, Delhi, 1863-64; secretary to the government of the Punjab, 1864-78, and was on two occasions temporarily in charge of the administration of the province; acted as foreign secretary to the government of India, 1876-77; additional member of governor-general's council, 1877-79; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1878-81; retired, 1881; fellow of University of Calcutta, 1875; vice-president, University College, Lahore, 1879.

THUILLIER, SIR HENRY EDWARD LANDOR, General, Kt., C.S.I., R.A., F.R.S., late Survey of India.—Educated at Addiscombe, 1831-32; first commission, Bengal artillery, Dec., 1832; arrived in India, 19th Oct., 1833; served in the survey department, from Dec., 1836, and conducted the surveys of Jynteah, the Pooroo division of Cuttack, and the district of Cachar, and part of Sylhet; while on furlough to England, 1844-46, was orderly officer of Warley barracks for a year; deputy surveyor-general of India, Jan., 1847, to March, 1861, when he was appointed surveyor-general of India; retired Dec., 1877; lieutenant-colonel, 1861; companion of the Star of India, May, 1870; knighted in 1879; major-general in 1870; lieutenant-general, July, 1879; general, July, 1881;

colonel commandant, R.A., Jan., 1883; joint author of the 'Manual of Survey for India.'

THUILLIER, HENRY RAVENSHAW, Colonel, R.E., Survey of India.—Addiscombe, Aug., 1855, to June, 1857; lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1857; arrived, in India, 29th April, 1859; appointed to the survey department as assistant in the great trigonometrical survey, May, 1859; deputy superintendent, Sept., 1869; officiating superintendent, great trigonometrical survey, April-Oct., 1876; 1st grade deputy superintendent, Oct., 1878; deputy surveyor-general in charge of the revenue survey branch, Oct., 1884; acting surveyor-general from Feb., 1886; was confirmed, Feb., 1887.

TIDY, WILLIAM MEYOTT, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1860; served in the N.W. Provinces from Aug., 1862, as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Sept., 1878; retired, 1886.

TINNEY, G. F., Registry Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th July, 1882.

TIRUVARUR MUTTUSWAMI AIYAR, SWAMINATHA AIYAR, B.A., Madras C.S.—Educated at Madras University; appointed probationer for the civil service, Nov., 1883; confirmed as a member of the Madras civil service, Jan., 1886, and appointed assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1887.

TODD-NAYLOR, HENRY PAUL, C.I.E., Bengal C.S.
